# Thread: Why must we see the sunrise? Sunlight must hit the membrane that houses Kreb's cycle to get it to spin correctly to inno
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1761424596358635655.html>
**Published:** 2024-02-24T14:40:33.000Z
**Tweets:** 15
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1761424596358635655>

Why must we see the sunrise? Sunlight must hit the membrane that houses Kreb's cycle to get it to spin correctly to innovate life. If it does not, disease or death results. It is that simple. What does sunlight do to Kreb's cycle? Every sunrise, they all spin in unison to remove H+ from the chemical intermediates to supply the mitochondrial matrix with its fuel source. Istopic LIGHT Hydrogen. Mitochondria are solar hydrogen furnaces.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1761430351866478639>

Without sunlight, what happens to the TCA cycle? The TCA cycle functions backward as it did over 2 billion years ago in bacteria that took H+ and CO2 from the atmosphere to make energy to survive. The counterclockwise flow of the TCA cycle can turn gases into life. This is especially favorable when there is no oxygen in the atmosphere.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1761436137682297302>

How do we reverse spin the TCA cycle? Remove oxygen or remove sunlight. We use ferrodoxin, an iron protein, to change the flow of the TCA cycle to accomplish it. This is when mitochondria consume H+ and CO2 to build things.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1761438557002961196>

Ferredoxins are small proteins containing iron and sulfur atoms organized as iron–sulfur clusters. These biological "capacitors" can accept or discharge electrons, with the effect of a change in the oxidation state of the iron atoms between +2 and +3. In this way, ferredoxin acts as an electron transfer agent in biological redox reactions.

Ferredoxins are one of the most reducing biological electron carriers. They typically have a midpoint potential of -420 mV. This is biophysically why they can stop the clockwise flow of metabolism to oxygen and force mitochondria to consume H+ and CO2 to make biochemical substrates. That level of charge exceeds delta psi on the inner mitochondrial membrane = 150-400mV

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1761439023518622198>

Some ferredoxins have a sufficiently high redox potential that can be directly reduced by NADPH. One such ferredoxin is adrenoxin (-274mV), which takes part in the biosynthesis of many mammalian steroids.

The ferredoxin Fd3 in the roots of plants that reduces nitrate and sulfite has a midpoint potential of -337mV and is also reduced by NADPH.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1761439289806627313>

Ferredoxin-1 in humans participates in the synthesis of thyroid hormones. It also transfers electrons from adrenodoxin reductase to CYP11A1, a CYP450 enzyme responsible for cholesterol side chain cleavage. FDX-1 has the capability to bind to metals and proteins.

Ferredoxin-2 participates in heme A and iron–sulphur protein synthesis in mammals

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1761439563476545876>

Human ferredoxins are

2Fe–2S: AOX1; FDX1; FDX2; NDUFS1; SDHB; XDH;
4Fe–4S: ABCE1; DPYD; NDUFS8;

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1761440634471125103>

As widespread and biochemically diverse as the heme proteins, the ferredoxins and related iron sulfur proteins differ from heme proteins in that they display a multitude of cluster nuclearities and coordination geometries.
Ferredoxins are small, mostly acidic, soluble proteins found ubiquitously in biological organisms. They possess a highly negative redox potential and use their iron-sulfur cluster to act as electron distributors in various metabolic pathways.

R Cammack Advances in Inorganic Chemistry, ed G Sykes (Academic, San Diego), pp. 1–322 (1992).

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1761442310791798942>

Ferredoxin, as a heme protein, responds to sunlight.
For example, in plants, photosystem I, excited by light of wavelength shorter than 700 nm, generates NADPH by forming reduced ferredoxin. Photosystem II, requiring light of wavelength shorter than 680 nm, produces a strong oxidant that includes O2.

In anaerobic bacteria, ferredoxin a is the Clostridium pasteurianum ferredoxin and generally occurs in all fermentative and nonphotosynthetic green anaerobic bacteria. Its absorption spectrum shows a single peak at 390 or 385 nm and a peak in the UV region at about 280 nm, with a shoulder at 300 nm.

[sciencedirect.com/topics/neurosc…](https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/neuroscience/ferredoxin)

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1761443717771055321>

These circumstances should tell us in medicine, there is a significant link between the type of ferredoxin present and the light environment in which we exist. This marries the light that life forms create via biophotons. It also means that anabolism and catabolism must link to sunlight, ferredoxin chemistry, and the biology of mTOR.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1761445558869238133>

Ferredoxin is an iron-based protein in humans. Iron is an essential cofactor with unique redox properties. Iron regulatory proteins 1 and 2 (IRP1/2) have been established as essential regulators of cellular iron homeostasis. Today, it is well known that the mammalian target of rapamycin (mTOR) regulates iron homeostasis by modulating the stability of transferrin receptor 1 (TfR1) and altering cellular iron flux. What remains black to centralized medicine is how mTOR acts as a photo-optical switch for the TCA cycle spin, counterclockwise or clockwise. Light controls this logic gate. Margaret Dayhoff began to use the ideas in Shannon's work to figure this mystery out. Today, centralized science still needs to learn what she really uncovered.

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1761447579311648873>

When you biohack the periodic table of elements, you realize that iron has some unique abilities. Iron can quickly gain and lose electrons, facilitating the production of reactive oxygen species (ROS) by the Fenton reaction (Aisen et al., 2001). Thus, iron levels must be tightly controlled at both cellular and systemic levels, and deregulation of iron homeostasis is associated with aging, metabolic disorders, and cancer (Aisen et al., 2001; Altamura and Muckenthaler, 2009; Torti and Torti, 2011; Weinberg, 2010).

Tight control implies photovoltaic sensors. That is what mTOR is. The sun is what informs what you do in your by creating metabolism. That, in turn, makes a spectrum of light from mitochondria. That light then controls the clockwise flow of metabolism. When mitochondria are defective or have a high heteroplasmy, the spectrum of light is different, and what becomes possible for the cell varies from the default state. This is how chronic diseases and aging occur. These are the things Lane, Attia, Huberman, and 99.99% of biochemists still do not know.

**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1761584333259718956>

What does sunlight also do to Kreb's cycle?  Every sunrise, they all spin in a clockwise direction in unison to remove H+ and move carbon from the chemical intermediates to supply the mitochondrial matrix with its fuel source.  Istopic LIGHT Hydrogen.  Mitochondria are solar hydrogen furnaces.

# Thread: Human perception and attention has significant limitations A.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1753001513348202695.html>
**Published:** 2024-02-03T01:12:41.000Z
**Tweets:** 8
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1753001513348202695>

Human perception and attention has significant limitations

A. Always pay attention to what you're missing in science and in nature when you’re ill. It will give you clues to trends you’ve missed. Our brains have limited mitochondrial capacity, because energy is a zero sum game based on Kleibers law. More often and we will fail to notice important details or events that fall outside our focus. Being aware of our limitations helps us become more vigilant and attentive & curious to what we might be overlooking.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1753002421578555443>

B. Always question your intuition - Our intuition is not always reliable, and it can lead us to make errors in judgment and decision-making. By questioning our intuitions, we can avoid falling into cognitive traps and make more rational choices.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1753003938020745556>

C. Try to be careful and cautious with multitasking - Our brains are not built by nature to be efficient at multitasking, and attempting to do so can lead to decreased cognitive performance. Human executive function has signing thermodynamic limits. Most are unaware of this and this is why our species suffers an increased likelihood of making errors. This is the basis of the Dinning Kruger effect. Recognizing the limitations of multitasking can help us prioritize and allocate our attention more effectively.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1753004769361211790>

D. Understand the power of expectations - Our expectations significantly shape our perceptions. The more you expect the more disappointed you’ll be if your expectations for someone or something falls short. They can lead us to see what we expect to see, even when the reality is different. Being aware of this bias can help us approach situations with a more open mind and consider alternative perspectives.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1753006066277797896>

E. Context matters a great deal in science and in medicine because every variable has its own relativity. This makes our perceptions and judgments to become heavily influenced by the context in which they occur. Recognizing the impact of context can help us avoid making snap judgments and consider a broader range of information before forming conclusions in science & medicine.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1753007048361410562>

F. Take cognitive breaks in nature to refresh and sharpen your senses to become more attentive. Our attention is a limited resource that our brain manufactures and it can become fatigued over time. Taking regular breaks, engaging in activities that relax and refresh our minds, can help sustain our attention and improve our overall cognitive performance. The sun CT and Forrest bathing help this.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1753008131536212079>

G. Challenge your assumptions in science. This is a critical skill to hypertrophy. It is not your muscles that should get large but the manner in which you think about the world that should expand.

We have cognitive biases that influence the way we interpret information. Actively seeking out alternative perspectives and questioning our assumptions can help us overcome these biases and make more informed decisions.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1753008871432425678>

H. You must become facile to embrace ambiguity and uncertainty - The world is full of complexities and uncertainties. Recognizing that we may not have all the answers can foster a sense of curiosity and openness to new information, allowing us to adapt and learn in an ever-changing environment. This is a critical step in personal sovereignty

Researchers have shown that when our skin is exposed to the sun's full spectrum rays, Nitric oxide is released into our microcirculation in our blood vessels, which helps lower blood pressure while ALSO slowing electron chain transport to decrease our need for food. The findings suggest that exposure to sunlight improves health overall because the benefits of reducing blood pressure far outweigh the risk of developing skin cancer. Full-spectrum sunlight also reduces the need for food, so it is the way nature asks us to restrict our calories naturally.

### Tweet 3

UVB/UVA, and the VDR receptor in mitochondria augment this "fasting effect" on our colony of mitochondria. Very few people understand how sunlight truly keeps us well.
Do not be a fool and think standing behind a pane of glass on a sunny day is wise. It is not. WHY?
Glass blocks all UVB and most UVA light. It blocks 30-60% of IR light and HAS no effect on blue light from the sun, so on a relative basis, standing behind glass inside means no Vitamin D3, markedly reduced nitric oxide production, which raises your BP, reduction of red to lowers your ATP production, and excessive amounts of blue through. ### Tweet 4

Production of this pressure-reducing compound - called nitric oxide - is separate from the body's manufacture of vitamin D3 from cholesterol in the skin, which rises after exposure to sunshine later in the day. UVA light and NO are made much earlier in the day. But you must have skin in the game to gain this benefit.
Until now, only Vitamin D3 had been thought to solely explain the sun's benefit to human health. In 1992, the Nobel Prize in medicine was given for discovering nitric oxide. Researchers studied the blood pressure of 24 volunteers who sat beneath tanning lamps for two sessions of 20 minutes each. In one session, the volunteers were exposed to both the lamps' UV rays and heat (IRA). In the other, the UV rays were blocked, so only the lamps' heat affected the skin. The results showed that blood pressure dropped significantly for one hour following exposure to UV rays but not after the heat-only sessions.

### Tweet 5

Scientists say that this shows that the sun's UVA rays lead to health benefits. The volunteers' vitamin D levels remained unaffected in both sessions.

We now need cardiologists to reject the "dermatologist evidence" about the sun to look at the relative risks of heart disease and skin cancer in people who have received different amounts of sun exposure.

# Thread: How we should look at food is like an onion.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1750918804106838478.html>
**Published:** 2024-01-26T00:31:14.000Z
**Tweets:** 4
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1750918804106838478>

How we should look at food is like an onion.  Each layer has a different idea and function buried in it.  Food fundamentally is an electromagnetic bar code at the mitochondrial level, but at the biochemical level, food takes on a different meaning for me as a decentralized clinician.

Fats are basically a chain of carbon and hydrogen; however, sugar from photosynthesis comes premixed with oxygen by Nature. The chemical formula of glucose and fructose is C6H12O6. Coming with premixed oxygen implies less water production from metabolism.  This has a huge effect on the weight of the mammal.  This is good news for bats, who are a mammal and who need to limit their weight to fulfill their superpower to fly.
 
This is a disadvantage when you have a big brain because every neuron needs to release water with its action potential to perform neurologic tasks.  This is why the preferred bat fuel isn't the same as the human food fuel. Did you know that bat's fuel source burns with a different type of flame than we do in their colony of mitochondria.

Below, examine the picture carefully. On the left, the flame is relatively oxygen-poor but very fuel-rich. It means it will burn longer to power more work. Its flame longevity is extended compared to the flames on the right. If we used the same molar concentrations of fuel, the burn rate varies, and this defines the amount of work that can be done in the system of the mammal. The trade-off is that it reduces the flame, and this causes it to appear more red. Humans are built to take full advantage of this color change with their non visual photoreceptors. This is a big deal for cholesterol in our lipid rafts. This is a big disadvantage for bats and their lung function. Fat metabolism produces more red light. Doing this Bunsen burner mitohack with a spectrascope will show you these facts to be true. Very few food gurus will understand this nuance.

It is not what you know that is critical about fuels. It is what you notice about them, that is.

Looking at Bunsen burner flames and the fuels we feed them in the lab teaches this lesson.

This is what separates the food guru from the decentralized thinker. Food is not what the biochemist thinks it is. It is not what you currently think it is either.

These facts greatly impacted my thinking about weight training in a gym under blue light when you are a mammal who does not have a body plan built around flying but thinking. On the right, most flames are oxygen-rich, oxidizing flames, appearing blue.  Glucose burns to create a blue flame.

Each carbon from fats in the human diet requires additional oxygen from the air to be oxidized to carbon dioxide and water. This could be like the difference between a flame, where combustion occurs when the fuel is not already mixed with oxygen from the air and when the fuel is already mixed with oxygen.  

This change produces a shorter, hotter, more intense flame to drive your mammalian body plan.  This is why the human brain needs fat, and bats like to use glucose to fly.  They are both mammals but do not use the same fuels, and their body plans are wildly different as a result.  Mammals adapt to the environment they are in.  The last 125 years have driven humans to be fatter due to counterintuitive environmental pressure due to the alien use of the electromagnetic spectrum of light. This means fat fuels, fat under their skin and in their viscera, may provide survival benefits if the world continues to be blue light and filled with RF and microwaves. That is food for thought for the smooth-brainer food gurus.

That is the size where life happens in cells. “Nuclear Quantum Effects in Hydrophobic Nanoconfinement” by Buddha Ratna Shrestha, Sreekiran Pillai, Adriano Santana, Stephen H. Donaldson Jr., Tod A. Pascal and Himanshu Mishra, 31 July 2019, Journal of Physical Chemistry Letters.

Food, Water, and neutrinos have a lot in common. How you experience neutrinos in life is not how you experience neutrino use in your mitochondria. The discovery that neutrinos have mass was groundbreaking. However, their absolute mass remains unknown. Neutrinoless double beta decay experiments aim to determine whether neutrinos are their own antiparticles and, if so, provide a means to determine the mass of the neutrino species involved.

Determining the mass through neutrinoless double beta decay experiments using 76Ge is only possible if scientists understand the properties of the decay of 76Ge into selenium-76 (76Se). A study published below in Physical Review C provides key input for these kinds of experiments. I just showed you my decentralized onion on food and fuels. Your food gurus are clueless. Horrible advice. Never use them. Skin exposure to NP-containing sunscreens leads to incorporation of TiO2 and ZnO NPs in the stratum corneum, which can alter specific NP attenuation properties due to particle–particle, particle–skin, and skin–particle–light physicochemical interactions. Both sunscreen NPs induce (photo)cyto- and genotoxicity and have been sporadically observed in viable skin layers especially in case of long-term exposures and ZnO. Photocatalytic effects, the highest for anatase TiO2, cannot be completely prevented by coating of the particles, but silica-based coatings are most effective. Caution should still be exercised when new sunscreens are developed and research that includes sunscreen NP stabilization, chronic exposures, and reduction of NPs’ free-radical production should receive full attention.

# Thread: So in cells is light a particle or a wave? Light is neither a wave nor a particle in the way that you intuitively think
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1747940491985907869.html>
**Published:** 2024-01-19T12:29:16.000Z
**Tweets:** 5
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1747940491985907869>

So in cells is light a particle or a wave?
Light is neither a wave nor a particle in the way that you intuitively think of those things: it's something more — but something which has properties and behaviors that occasionally match up with one of these categories.
Light is neither a wave nor a particle — it is something that is both more complicated, but also much simpler and more elegant.

Light is the manifestation of elementary excitations (quanta) of an underlying quantum field: the Electromagnetic Field. These fields are described by a branch of physics called Quantum Field theory. We call these elementary excitations “photons” in QFT.

These excitations are neither waves nor (classical) particles — but rather an entirely separate category of object altogether. However, they do sometimes exhibit behavior associated with either waves or particles — and depending on the environment/circumstances they exist in they might act more like one than the other.
Skin/eyes in the game.

It is decentralized medicine for all with no patent or commercial or shareholders behind it. It's free for those that think for themselves or have trust in a clinician to guide them to their optimal self.

# Thread: The course to constructing a 2024 vision is rarely going to be linear.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1739250375713067398.html>
**Published:** 2023-12-14T03:00:35.000Z
**Tweets:** 9
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1739250375713067398>

The course to constructing a 2024 vision is rarely going to be linear. Failure is a natural section of the journey toward constructing a vision. I view failure and chaos as precious opportunities. They tell me where the pathway in the minefield lies. Your 2024 vision will require adaptability, discipline, and resilience. Adaptability and a willingness to change will help you navigate challenges and capture surprising opportunities. Stay dedicated to your goals, even when confronted with boundaries or distractions. Develop habits that guide your imaginative and prescient and domesticate a fine mind-set to overcome challenges.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1739251505557803332>

in 2024 you begin to question the explanations they have been given and seek to uncover the truth about if freedom exists in finance and medicine. Their pursuit of knowledge and understanding will highlight the importance of your critical thinking to challenge established narratives. Your sovereignty and agency matter. Your desire for autonomy and self-determination will reinforce the importance of individual expression and the pursuit of personal fulfillment.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1739252374030418039>

Social media creates a mental chatter in your head. Your attention to it determines what it is capable of in your life. Unplug from the chatter. This "voice in your head," is your constant stream of thoughts from your daily interactions with media. This voice often creates negativity and emotional distress. When you understand what it is capable of you can detach from it and find inner peace by hypertrophying your thinking.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1739254603059695690>

No matter who you are, recognize that you are already worthy and loved, regardless of your mistakes or imperfections. Finding those people who do care for you should be a priority in 2024. There will be turbulence developing in the fiat world this year and you will need to rely on more eyes than your own two to navigate.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1739255320377987257>

Tap your passion in 2024. Passion involves the full use of your faculties. It requires the precision of knowing when to deploy your talents. It involves the activation of one's potential in tasks that seem to make time contract. You produce for you and not the audience. Focus on the intrinsic reward of the activity itself, rather than seeking validation or approval from others.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1739257027774296301>

In 2024 escape the hustle of your big city for a vacation in a remote rental home. While there spend most of your time outside. Unplug from your life for 4 days.

If the the lack of information about the world during this retreat cause you to grapple with fear, uncertainty, and a sense of isolation, you have just shown that technology is a problem for your brain. Change that.

If you do not, enjoy the default reset Nature provides you. Try to do it more times this year. Maybe one a quarter.

Isolation can be something that opens the door to heaven or hell for you. It is your job to find out which one it opens.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1739259961362801079>

In 2024 change this list.

a. Begin to advocate for yourself.
b. Stop apologizing for everything or avoiding conflict
c. Don't settle for less than you deserve.
d. Communicate to express yourself clearly
e. Become concise and precise
f. Become persuasive in your arguments
g. Decline tasks & commitments you're not aligned with
h. Stop being a 'yes man' for your friend group.
i. Showcase your value to all
j. Spend time with those who value your time.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1739264342682443802>

For the ladies. Embrace solar maintence of your bits.
Try to eliminate your GYN from your life permanently by adapting in this daily routine in 2024. Make some time everyday for your lady bits.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1739264811186176073>

Wake up about your privacy and data abuse. Governments are going to try to convince the world the CBDC is no harm. They lie about everything to us.

Become keenly aware of the ethical implications of data collection and the potential for abuse when personal information is misused. Anyone who does not protect your privacy should be cut from your life in 2024.

# Thread: NIR light can achieve green light-responsive photolysis with a single-photon process.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1733057741327908922.html>
**Published:** 2023-12-02T13:05:07.000Z
**Tweets:** 12
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1733057741327908922>

NIR light can achieve green light-responsive photolysis with a single-photon process. Be aware that Pharma is attempting to use light to create prescriptions for diseases they helped create. Be aware Big Pharma is getting into the light game to try to improve the effectiveness of bad drugs. You need to understand why they are doing it for diseases like cancer. Many new cases of cancer are linked to SV40 contamination.

@twocitizenships @RobertKennedyJr

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1733060501804925037>

It is highly likely in the next few years decentralized research will show that 380 nm light action on the mTOR complex changes the chemistry of this regulator to prevent cancer from developing metabolically in a redox shift.

Why?

We now have new data that reveals shifting the mTOR ensemble toward the catalysis-favored state will hinder cancer growth. Light is capable of altering chemical activations via photo catalytic conversions using optical photonics mechanisms.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1733103135307047207>

Nematic liquid crystals represent the basic form of compounds. The elongated nematic molecule is optically anisotropic and is characterized by a uniaxial optical index. The birefringence of the molecule is positive, and its optic axis (extraordinary refractive index) is coincident with the molecular long axis.

Below, you can see the spontaneous organization of the structures in a nematic liquid crystal, where birefringence is equal to ne - n0 and is a function of the applied voltage. This is how sunlight self-organizes liquid crystalline matter inside of us to operate.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1733105023293268189>

Another form of liquid crystals is cholesteric liquid crystals. These are generated by adding chiral twisting agents to the nematic liquid crystal, generating a twist in the nematics, and creating a molecular tilt that follows a helical pattern from one layer to the next. The planes of the orientation of the optical director for the liquid crystals change orientation periodically, as shown below, where P is the pitch of the cycle.

When you understand that all liquid crystals have all these physical forms that do things to light, you have to ask yourself the question why would Mother Nature go to all this trouble of building all this cool stuff inside of a cell if cells emit no light? It turns out, @hubermanlab that the proof of endogenous light production has been staring at us from the microscope for 150 years, but we were too ignorant to understand what the hell we were looking at.

Light created inside the cell with the wavelength and a specific circular polarization that matches this pitch will be strongly scattered by the liquid crystal. The liquid crystal has no effect on waves with circular polarization handedness opposite to the molecular plane orientation. This tells you that cells polarize light made from mitochondrial metabolism. It also tells you that there is another way for unpolarized light from the sun to still pass through tissues to get to their destination without altering the endogenous light signal with an interference pattern. Why? All other wavelengths pass unaffected. Imagine that.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1733106649152262602>

In the future, decentralized clinicians I work with will learn that these liquid crystalline characteristics are great redox health markers. Why? Liquid crystals are flexoelectric because of their optical properties. nnEMF alters these properties in a myriad of ways. I just talked about some of these with @bitcoinand\_beef on a podcast not yet released.

Flexoelectricity defines the linear coupling between the mechanical strain gradient with the electric polarization or the mechanical response under the electric field gradient. This unique electromechanical coupling mechanism allows the measurement of strain gradient information in mechanical structures electrically, which is regarded as a sensitive indicator of the structural health status. Imagine that!

Flexoelectricity in nematic liquid crystals is a well-studied field in liquid crystal science (e.g., de Gennes and Prost 1993, Chandrasekhar 1992).

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1733111381094735881>

Experimentally, Patel and Meyer (1987) investigated flexoelectricity in cholesterics by applying a static electric field (E), perpendicular to the helix axis. This leads to a periodic splay–bend pattern in the helix, which allows the determination of the flexoelectric coefficient (see Nematic Liquid Crystals: Flexoelectricity for more).

This is another failure mode for cellular condensed soft matter semiconductors inside of cells. It is a topological failure.

An analysis by Brand and Pleiner (1997) shows that the situation in the geometry initially studied by Patel and Meyer is, rather complicated (below). That does not stop my tribe. We are black swans and we love the chaos of difficulty.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1733112814191968596>

Brand and Pleiner found that the flexoelectric contribution to the generalized energy is a surface effect = topological effect. Second, depending on the boundary conditions and geometry one can find experimentally a rotation of the optical axis that is either present or absent. Such a rotation of the optical axis with respect to the laboratory frame set by the helix in the field-free case and the external field was found by Patel and Meyer.

Features that add to the complexity of the behavior of cholesteric liquid crystals in an electric field are the dynamic effects coupling director orientation to the external electric field and its gradients. There is a dissipative analog of the flexoelectric effect already in nematic liquid crystals (Brand and Pleiner 1984) which act as a contribution to the dissipation function takes the form below

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1733113923019178095>

The situation becomes even more complex in cholesteric liquid crystals due to the above mentioned Lehmann-type effects. In the dissipation function related to electric effects, one has the cross-coupling term below.

It must be emphasized that this Lehmann-type dissipative cross-coupling contains even one gradient less than the dynamic analog of the flexoelectric effect. A detailed analysis was done by Brand and Pleiner in 1997.

It showed that the term ∼ψE is crucial for interpreting the experiments performed by Patel and Meyer in 1987.

While Patel and Meyer just minimized the sum of the elastic and the flexoelectric energies to deduce the flexoelectric coefficient in a cholesteric, Brand and Pleiner showed that there is no static, but rather a stationary solution only, which also involves the Lehmann-type term ∼ψE.

Consequently, it is impossible to extract e1+e3 from these experiments without knowledge of ψE. Sounds like Heisenberg's uncertainty principle in cells is active.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1733114698290147615>

Last but not least is the queer biological transition, where chemicals can move from cis to trans by light.

The trans-cis isomerization reaction plays a vital role in several photoactive biological pigments. The vision process is perhaps the best-known example, where absorption of a photon by the retinal Shiff base chromophore induces a cis-to-trans isomerization of the chromophore as the primary reaction.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1733115515810247040>

Some nematic liquid crystals are azo-compounds, i.e., their molecular structures are analogous to the typical one depicted below. An azo-compound when irradiated at its absorption wavelength, will change from the trans to the cis form.

Trans–cis isomerization of these azo-nematic liquid crystals or azo-dopants in NLCs could also give rise to a refractive index change through modification of the NLC's order parameter and/or director axis reorientation. The trans-species are oblong in shape and aligned parallel to the director axis.
On the other hand, the ‘bent’ structure of the cis species is incongruous to the nematic order and thus causes an order parameter change and, therefore, a refractive index modification of light in the cell changing physiologic behavior. Blue light is a topological disruptor here in the retina.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1733117594423464330>

Such refractive index changes in cells are owed to optical changes called order parameter modification and are similar to thermal effects discussed in my blogs on the topic. The response \[on-\] time for these trans–cis isomerization-induced order-parameter changes can be pretty fast (as fast as nanoseconds in some cases). The relaxation time (typically in the millisecond regime) is decided by the director axis relaxation rate, as discussed in my blogs.

The other index-changing mechanism is the tendency of the trans molecule to align with its transition moment perpendicular to the optical polarization to minimize the interaction (absorption) energies. This causes the NLC director axis to realign through the intermolecular torque effects. Both mechanisms are characterized by a nonlinear index coefficient n2 on the order of 10^−3 cm2 W^−1.

Cholesterol Vitamin D transition uses a photoisomerization step. Why is Vitamin D further converted to active components elsewhere (kidney/liver)? The light-induced isomerization is usually followed by a series of dark reactions, which ultimately result in the generation of a nerve impulse that is transmitted to the brain.

Other biological processes where the primary light-induced reaction is believed to be an isomerization reaction are the light-driven proton pump of bacteriorhodopsin in the bacterium Halobacterium halobium and the light sensory process in green plants mediated by the pigment phytochrome.

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1733117882274349448>

END OF LESSON

# Thread: Today's biophysics lesson: Technology makes you mentally ill when used to excess.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1730562297947471966.html>
**Published:** 2023-12-02T13:05:07.000Z
**Tweets:** 9
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1730562297947471966>

Today's biophysics lesson: Technology makes you mentally ill when used to excess.

The major non-thermal effect of nnEMF is a net increase of deuterium in the TCA cycle with a change in the pool of hydrogen in the NADPH pool and a loss of melatonin, which causes more deuterium in the cells. This single change destroys cell membranes where DHA is located. DHA controls the lipid cytosocial regions on lipid rafts in cell membranes to perform the movement of electrons on a liquid crystalline semiconductive circuit. The most important one connects the retina to the SCN to control all growth metabolism in animals because it controls the circadian mechanism, which determines how the clock genes work everywhere in your body. When you live in a nnEMF world, say like musicians, you become depressed and quickly collect deuterium in your matrix, and you might kill yourself or become addicted to opiates. We've recently seen it in Prince, the King of Pop, Houston, Winehouse, Cobain, Cornwell, and Bennington. "Non-thermal microwave/lower frequency electromagnetic fields (EMFs) act via voltage-gated calcium channel (VGCC) activation. Calcium channel blockers block EMF effects, and several types of additional evidence confirm this mechanism. Low-intensity microwave EMFs have been proposed to produce neuropsychiatric effects, sometimes called microwave syndrome, and the focus of this review is whether these are indeed well documented and consistent with the known mechanism(s) of action of such EMFs. VGCCs occur in very high densities throughout the nervous system and have near-universal roles in releasing neurotransmitters and neuroendocrine hormones. Soviet and Western literature shows that much of the impact of non-thermal microwave exposures in experimental animals occurs in the brain and peripheral nervous system, such that nervous system histology and function show diverse and substantial changes. These may be generated through roles of VGCC activation, producing excessive neurotransmitter/neuroendocrine release and oxidative/nitrosative stress and other responses. Excessive VGCC activity has been shown from genetic polymorphism studies to have roles in producing neuropsychiatric changes in humans. Two U.S. government reports from the 1970s to 1980s provide evidence of neuropsychiatric effects of non-thermal microwave EMFs based on occupational exposure studies. 18 more recent epidemiological studies provide substantial evidence that microwave EMFs from cell/mobile phone base stations, excessive cell/mobile phone usage, and from wireless smart meters can each produce similar patterns of neuropsychiatric effects, with several of these studies showing clear dose–response relationships. Lesser evidence from 6 additional studies suggests that short wave, radio station, occupational, and digital TV antenna exposures may produce similar neuropsychiatric effects. Among the more commonly reported changes are sleep disturbance/insomnia, headache, depression/depressive symptoms, fatigue/tiredness, dysesthesia, concentration/attention dysfunction, memory changes, dizziness, irritability, loss of appetite/body weight, restlessness/anxiety, nausea, skin burning/tingling/dermographism, and EEG changes.

In summary, then, the mechanism of action of microwave EMFs, the role of the VGCCs in the brain, the impact of non-thermal EMFs on the brain, extensive epidemiological studies performed over the past 50 years, and five criteria testing for causality, all collectively show that various non-thermal microwave EMF exposures produce diverse neuropsychiatric effects. BOOM! If you have a mental disease, you have a bad relationship with the electromagnetic spectrum.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1730564204988444907>

All stress leads to this ------>

Lactate--not glucose--is the primary energy substrate in ALL mammals. You should read that again, FOLKS. Now you know that the levels of Vitamin C in the glial cells are linked to the amount of catecholamine made. Is dopamine a catecholamine? YEP. Is dopamine levels related to creativity? YEP. Where is the lactate glucose paper, Uncle Jack? …

Glucose is the monosaccharide utilized by most eukaryotes to generate metabolic energy, and in the majority of eukaryotic systems, glycolysis is the first biochemical pathway where glucose breaks down via a series of enzymatic reactions to produce relatively small amounts of adenosine triphosphate (ATP). In 1940, the sequence of these glycolytic reactions was elucidated, a breakthrough recognized as the first such elucidation of a biochemical pathway in history. Accordingly, the glycolytic breakdown of glucose ends up either with pyruvate as the final product under aerobic conditions or with lactate, to which pyruvate is reduced under anaerobic conditions. Consequently, pyruvate has been designated and is held to be the substrate of the mitochondrial tricarboxylic acid cycle, where it is completely oxidized into CO2 and H2O, while lactate has been defined and being held to as a useless dead-end product, poisonous at times, of which cells must discard off quickly.

More than four decades after the glycolytic pathway has been elucidated, studies of both muscle and brain tissues have suggested that lactate is not necessarily a useless end product of anaerobic glycolysis and may actually play a role in bioenergetics. These studies have shown that muscle and brain tissues can oxidize and utilize lactate as a mitochondrial energy substrate. These results have been met with great skepticism, but a large number of publications over the past quarter of a century have strengthened the idea that lactate does play an essential and, possibly, crucial role in energy metabolism. These findings have shed light on a major drawback of the originally proposed aerobic version of the glycolytic pathway, that is, its inability to regenerate nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide (oxidized form) (NAD+), as opposed to anaerobic glycolysis that features the cyclical ability of the glycolytic lactate dehydrogenase (LDH) system to regenerate NAD+ upon pyruvate reduction to lactate. The free radical signal of cytochrome one is made by superoxide. This free radical is important to keep humming if you want optimal creativity in your neurons.

An examination of scientific investigations on carbohydrate metabolism of brain tissue in the 1920s and 1930s has already revealed that lactate can be readily oxidized. However, due to the prevailing centralized dogma of the carnivore bro science crew, according to which lactate is a waste product, its oxidation was assumed to be a possible mechanism of elimination. This chapter examines old and new research data on glucose glycolysis in muscle and brain tissues. This chapter consolidates the available data in an attempt to form a more accurate and clear description of this universal and very important bioenergetic chain of reactions.

Now, the story is complete, where creativity and chaos are linked.

This opsin is one of the more common ones in human ejaculation. This opsin is expressed in both neuronal (retina and brain) and epithelial tissues (cornea and skin). Neurectoderm tissues and gamete. NO regulates sperm capacitation, acrosomal reaction, and sperm motility and may also have an anti-apoptotic effect. He was able to solve the problem by seeing the invisible before anyone else could. In a way, our Center is a lot like Dr. Shannon. Shannon gave Bell Labs the proper ass-kicking when he wrote a paper in 1948 about using mathematics to control data. The paper, "A Mathematical Theory of Communication" really is what MY Quantum thermodynamics thesis is on my Patreon blog. Nobody really understands these parallels today because no one seems to realize that Quantum thermodynamics is the mathematics that dictates how nature processes energy and information in living systems. He asked what was the minimum requirement needed to create a message that could still be deciphered well. The equation he came up with looks identical to Boltzmann's equation for entropy.

He did this by actually figuring out how to measure the information in the messages. At Kruse Longevity Farm, we've done the same thing with mitochondrial biology, yet the world is sleeping on us. Shannon realized that the quantity of the message had ZERO to do with its true meaning. The same thing is true with the Warburg shift. Instead, messages in cells wholly relate to how unusual the message as compared to the standard boilerplate. He came up with a way to quantify this using a pattern of numbers. Using a "one and zero" is all it took to solve Bell labs' problems around understanding information.

This was how the binary code was discovered for digital computing and it was the beginning of information theory for humanity. He coined the term bit for information theorists. Qubit is what cells use. Photons have a quantum spin number that mimics the 1 and 0's in computer code.

At Kruse Longevity Center we've discovered a similar code in the living system that uses optical coding of Orbital angular momentum. The Rosetta stone of health is linked to understanding is the bare minimum a cell can tolerate to still properly function. Light signaling in cells appears to require mitochondrial redox power to be maintained between -200mV and -400mV in our colony of mitochondria. Frequencies and OAM are the keys.

That code involved the electrons and protons in mitochondria and how their signals are handled using their quantum spin state. Their spin state mimics the 1's and 0's of Claude Shannon. The spins of electrons/protons are not only manipulated by magnetic fields (ATPase of mitochondria) but also by electrical fields (protein side chains) and can be used to collect and store information from electrons or the photons they carry. All magnetic drives use spintronics today to magnetically store data on hard drives on digital computers.

At our center, we've uncovered how mitochondria do this with quantum spin states. You might want to pay attention to us.........we think we're going to change the world of medicine just like Claude Shannon changed the digital age. Understanding business test cases can help you become a Black Swan mitochondriac. Small examples below in pictures of what the world of Big tech is doing to all of you below your perception.

Almost every cell of every organism has its own little clock, but what makes it tick? How does it control time using light and information quanta?

In 1971, Seymour Benzer, a professor at the California Institute of Technology in the US, and his graduate student Ronald Konopka exposed fruit flies to ethyl methylsulfonate to create errors in their DNA. They created mutants that behaved like they had evolved on different planets, worlds with rotation periods of 19 or 27 hours or without intrinsic light and dark cycles. Remarkably, they could show that a single gene, a specific piece of DNA, was responsible for this change. How do you change DNA like this? The free radical signal of the mitochondria changes that state, which is changed by the light that is made by mtDNA in biophoton form. That is the biological version of the butterfly effect.

This was the first time behavior rather than physical characteristics, such as eye color or wing shape, had been linked to a gene. Their paper only got 9 citations in the literature in a DECADE. Yet, in 2017, they won the Nobel Prize for understanding how this one gene, called PER, was the key to understanding the clock and how information would be handled. That is how light creates creativity. The light alters the free radical signaling, and this changes how much dopamine and melatonin are made in the neuron using the spin state of the radical, O2 levels, and the movement of lactate to and fro. GOT it? Your diet gurus just keep shitting the bed with their drivel. END OF LESSON

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1727779897760731496>

LIGHT TRUMPS FOOD 100% of the time. I am glad it offends everyone. I could care less. NATURE rule not mine. Researchers are now beginning to go back and look at old badly done food/diet/cholesterol heart studies that had no good light controls to see what the effect on light was on the cohort as it continues to age.  The old Chicago Heart Association Detection Project in Industry was one such heart study done in 1967-1973.  A new study from Northwestern University decided to go back and look at the 1,395 survivors who were between 65-84 years old to see if light played a major role in disease generation.

The old failed cholesterol/heart disease study looked at 4 risk factors:

serum total cholesterol level <200 mg/dL
no use of cholesterol-lowering medication
blood pressure 120/≤80 mm Hg with no use of antihypertensive medication;
no current smoking; and no history of diabetes or heart attack.

The new Chicago Healthy Aging Study (CHAS) done by Northwestern University studied the survivors and shed light on a concerning trend: elderly individuals sleeping with lights on face doubled risks of obesity, diabetes, and high blood pressure. Imagine that.  Light not food increased the risk for diseases normally linked to the diet disease hypothesis of the 20th centrury dominated by centralized medicine.

The Chicago Healthy Aging Study revealed a startling truth I have highlighted for 20 years in decentralized medicine—consistent exposure to electric lights at night disrupts circadian rhythms, impacting our health by causing mitochondrial diseases.  Light induces thermodynamic changes that directly lead to chronic diseases that doctors drugs cannot fix.  Why?  Drugs cannot fix bad choices around modern lighting. This is why in El Salvador we are looking to rebuild the lighting industry that the US just destroyed in favor of the LED energy saving blub industry that destroys mitochondrial health.  It's time to dim the all the lights at night and embrace total darkness and prioritize restful sleep. This is especially true for those with mental illness.  Mental diseases result most when sunlight is blocked during the day and lights are used at night interupting the total darkness we need to recycle our mitochondrial engines to maintain health.  I'd suggest you read the eye-opening study, 'Light at night in older age is associated with obesity, diabetes, and hypertension,' and let's make a change together.  I know we will be doing this in the Ark being built in El Salvador.  No one seems to be interested in the NIH in my work at all. That will change soon too.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1727879676838674775>

How does the sun link to endocrinology and how does the photo endocrine programming link to the nuclear genome to alter methylation of the DNA via the epigenomic software created by mitochondria in cells? Vitamin D and Parathyroid Hormone are an example of how this process operates in humans.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1727880149150978532>

Vitamin D, synthesized in the skin, and parathyroid hormone (PTH), secreted by the parathyroid glands, increase serum Ca2+ concentrations via actions of cells from the changes in mitochondria on the gut, kidney, and bone. These two calcemic hormones regulate cytochrome p450 27B1 (CYP27B1) gene expression directly. Remember the P450 system is a heme based photoreceptor. This is how the sun alters the system inside by affecting the skin biology.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1727882266737516938>

PTH activates CYP27B1 transcription through stimulation of protein kinases A and C (PKA and PKC), whereas vitamin D, through binding to its nuclear receptor, VDR, represses CYP27B1 transcription. the VDR receptor slows ECT speeds while red light in the sun continues to keep the ATPase spinning to move protons EVEN though the flow of electrons from food or the beta-oxidation of our fat stores slows. This photonic protein wiring diagram reflects the vitamin D-mediated repression of the CYP27B1 gene, which results in rapid methylation of CpG sites on DNA and his is induced by vitamin D in this gene promoter. This methylation step requires DNMT1 and DNMT3B to be operational. Conversely, treatment with PTH causes active CpG demethylation of the CYP27B1 promoter. Thus, recent data shows results obtained with the CYP27B1 promoter suggest that DNA methylation/demethylation plays an important role in hormonal regulation of transcription. These are two of the many control arms of the epigenome in humans operational in sunlight. Methylation is a C-terminus event that i just spoke about in the Patreon blogs. This is critical in long covid cases and most clinicians have no idea how this effects mitochondrial function.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1727883124535681179>

The environment is the key to epigenomic programming in cells and C and N terminus events are how this happens.

Environmental factors induce numerous changes in DNA methylation, including imprinted genes involved in development. Regarding smoking, SP1 transcription factor activation by the nicotinic acetylcholine receptors and the increase of S-adenosylmethionine (the cell’s methyl donor) in response to hypoxia, as well as HIF-1α and methionine adenosyltransferase 2A, are involved.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1727883653894615219>

This is why astronauts have such different results in space and on Earth when they travel to each nnEMF environment.

Here is a small take on what happens to the N & C terminus in humans in space. [instagram.com/p/CzaCc-AP1Sk/](https://www.instagram.com/p/CzaCc-AP1Sk/)

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1727884447347872079>

It is important to note that the list of environmental factors capable of inducing epigenetic errors seems to be greatly underestimated. The most responsive change agent appears to be non-ionizing electromagnetic radiation on proteins given the latest NTP study on RF radiation risks in kids and adults using technology. This was released in 2018 in November.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1727884717981143273>

Epigenetics is defined as heritable changes in gene activity and expression that occur without alteration in DNA sequence. Histone modifications have been shown to function as a code that coordinates the recruitment of effector proteins and regulates chromatin structure, gene expression, and genome stability. DNA methylation is a biochemical process that begins with the solar programming of methyl donor groups. B12 and folate are two such photoreceptor in humans that appears to be critical to epigenetic program changes in humans. Methylation epigenetics concerns itself where a methyl group is added to the cytosine DNA nucleotides. Maintenance of DNA methylation by DNA methyltransferases is essential for normal mammalian development and long-term transcriptional silencing. The conversion of 5-methylcytosine to 5-hydroxymetyhylcytosine by ten–eleven translocation enzymes (TETs) is the initial step of active DNA demethylation. Many diseases have been associated with differentially methylated regions, and demethylating agents or methyl donors are being evaluated as epigenetic therapies. Sunlight is likely the cheapest and most effective ones.

End of the light lesson

Successful people don't wait for opportunities; they create them. Saying no to things establishes a focus for success. ### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1725720007315751135>

The wide frequency range of interaction with EMF is the functional characteristic of a fractal antenna in physics. Most need to appreciate that DNA appears to possess the two structural characteristics of fractal antennas: electronic conduction and self-symmetry. These properties contribute to greater reactivity of DNA with EMF in the environment, and the DNA damage likely is magnified by mt DNA damage that coincides except 3 times faster because it is more susceptible as a bacterial genome. This mitochondrial change likely accounts for increases in cancer epidemiology and variations in the rates of chemical evolution in early geologic history. Here, we see how light physics alters life's biology and chemistry.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1725724628411093457>

Quasiparticles and collective excitations (which are closely related) are emergent phenomena that occur when a microscopically complicated system such as a solid behaves as if it contained different weakly interacting particles in free space. Biomatter that does this is chlorophyll, hemoglobin, and melanin. For example, as an electron travels through a semiconductor, its motion is disturbed in a complex way by its interactions with all of the other electrons and nuclei; however, it approximately behaves like an electron with a different mass (effective mass) traveling unperturbed through free space above the semiconductor in its pi-electron cloud.  This traveling changes its mass on a relative basis. This “electron” with a different mass is called an “electron quasiparticle.” In another example, the aggregate motion of electrons in the valence band of a semiconductor is the same as if the semiconductor contained instead positively charged quasiparticles called holes, I mentioned above. Other quasiparticles or collective excitations include phonons (particles derived from the vibrations of atoms in a solid), plasmons (particles derived from plasma oscillations), and many others.  A “hole” is created because light has ejected an electron from that space to make the “hole”, and this open spot has the opposite charge of an electron.  It is more positive because the electrons have been ejected by a light photon, so a negative charge is lost.  An exciton is an electron and its hole bound together as a unit.

During the process of photoionization, the ejection of an electron will result in the formation of a positive ion (M+). The M+ is the hole.  The energy required to cause the ejection of an electron is known as ionization energy or electron binding energy.

Life is made up of cells filled with semiconductors, as Szent-Gyorgyi theorized in 1941 and Becker proved by experiment in the 1960’s.  An exciton is a bound state of an electron and an electron hole, which are attracted to each other by the electrostatic Coulomb force. It is an electrically neutral “quasiparticle” that exists in insulators, semiconductors, and some liquids.

Quasiparticles act like “holes in reality” that can exist even when nothing is really there.   In this way something odd occurs with respect to “time”.  This mechanism tells living things something essential about the “present time” and its boundaries are at the ledge of classical science.  It would help if you had “holes in reality” to understand how quantum time works in living things.  

Excitons are just one form of a quasiparticle that life uses that is deeply involved in time creation and energy generation.  Excitons are critical to photosynthesis, so they are germane to understanding the entire food web.  These particles are found in “holes” in things that exist in cells.  This method of energy generation is critical to life’s origins because it used this mechanism to build the entire food web on Earth.  If that sounds counterintuitive, welcome how nature uses sunlight quantum mechanically.

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1725838170573463984>

If you have an inflammatory condition, learn how to stimulate your own vagus nerve to curb it. This is done by controlling your breathing, circulation, gut and temperature. Vagus nerve stimulation dramatically reduces inflammation.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1725838293554663669>

The inflammatory reflex is a neural circuit that regulates the immune response to injury and invasion. All reflexes have an afferent and efferent arc. The Inflammatory reflex has a sensory afferent arc, activated by cytokines, and a motor or efferent arc, which transmits action potentials in the vagus nerve to suppress cytokine production. Increased signaling in the efferent arc inhibits inflammation and prevents organ damage.

It has also been shown that the brain can use this circuit not only to regulate the immune response but also to extend the immunological memory

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1725838877531754806>

The molecular basis of cytokine-inhibiting signals requires the neurotransmitter acetylcholine and the Alpha-7 nicotinic receptor receptor expressed on cytokine-producing cells. For example, the release of acetylcholine in the spleen suppresses the production of TNF and other cytokines, which causes damaging inflammation. Signaling in the efferent arc of the inflammatory reflex, termed the "Cholinergic anti-inflammatory pathway," provides a regulatory check on the innate immune system response to invasion and injury. The action potentials that arise in the vagus nerve are transmitted to the spleen, where a subset of specialized T cells are activated to secrete acetylcholine. The net effect of the reflex is to prevent the damage caused by excessive cytokine production.

Rosas-Ballina M, Ochani M, Parrish WR, et al. (August 2008). "Splenic nerve is required for cholinergic antiinflammatory pathway control of TNF in endotoxemia". Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences. 105 (31): 11008–13. Bibcode:2008PNAS..10511008R. doi:10.1073/pnas.0803237105. PMC 2504833. PMID 18669662.

### Tweet 18
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1725857660753391895>

Serotonin is a protein made from an aromatic amino acid, and it must be programmed by full-spectrum UV sunlight. When you are in full-spectrum artificial light, you deplete your body and brain of serotonin. Serotonin is made in the eyes, skin, and gut in humans.
The serotonin (5-HT) expression in the retina was first reported in the 1960s. Detecting vesicular monoamine transporter and serotonin receptors in several retinal cells confirms that 5-HT plays a neuromodulatory role in this structure. Whereas signaling pathways activated by 5-HT receptor binding have been poorly investigated, numerous data demonstrated that 5-HT is involved in retinal physiology, retinal physiopathology, and photoreceptor survival.
The eye is a remarkable photosensor that can detect a single photon and transmit its signal to the brain. The receptors for light in the vertebrate eye are the visual, photoreceptor cells of the retina. Each visual cell comprises two principal parts: the outer segment, which contains light-absorbing visual pigments, and the inner segment, which includes the nucleus, mitochondria, and other subcellular organelles and which metabolically support the functions of the outer part. The segments are connected by the ciliary process or cilium. The inner segments of visual cells have terminals that synapse with horizontal cells and bipolar cells. The bipolar cells, in turn, form synapses with ganglion and amacrine cells. All these cells have the enzymes that create serotonin, which is done by tryptophan hydroxylase.

It turns out serotonin is essential for keeping melanopsin damage in the retina and arteries from occurring. This is one of the vital undiagnosed things in C-19/long covid that few people realize leads to clinical deterioration.

What happens as a result? You become unable to handle acute stresses. The absence of serotonin in the brain alters acute stress responsiveness by interfering with the genomic function of the glucocorticoid receptors. When these receptors are altered, they no longer work as they should. When this process begins in the eye and ends in the brainstem, humans get a condition called adrenal fatigue, where it appears there is a global dysfunction of how their hormone receptors operate. Mammals skin are loaded with hair and hair has many formats of melanin in them. Melanin is a neuroectodermal derivative.

What does the visible spectrum of sun do to atoms after sunlight is turned into a laser by the neuroectoderm of mammals in their skins? When we go to a lab and fired a short pulse from a laser at ANY atom, it converts ANY atom into a positively charged ion to create "solvation actions" that alter biochemical probabilities below. Biophysics 101.

Solvation is the dissolving of a solute in a solvent—when salt dissolves in water, for example. The action does not stop just because the solute has dissolved; instead, the solvents continue to interact with the material that has been dissolved. So this process can be driven forward by laser light if there is a mechanism present in the living being that allows for it.

Get your skin in the game of Nature. Your clade, mammals, has this ability.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1722649824397345102>

Everyone says CO2 from humans is bad, yet, evolutionary history says CO2 was massively beneficial for primate evolution and we are the last standing primate. So how much do you know about CO2?

Probably not enough.

Frustrated Lewis pairs (FLPs) are capable of activating small molecules, like CO2 and convert it into value added products. Do you know what FLPs do to hydrogen atoms used in mitochondria?

A frustrated Lewis pair in chemistry is a compound or mixture containing a Lewis acid and a Lewis base that, because of steric hindrance, cannot combine to form a classical adduct.

Because of their "unquenched" reactivity, systems that employ FLPs are reactive toward substrates that can undergo heterolysis. For example, many FLPs split hydrogen molecules. The discovery that some FLPs split H2 triggered a rapid growth of research into FLPs.

Does your centralized experts know about this?

REALITY: FLPs allow for the formation of a new two-electron bond in many reactions that proceeds via electron migration of a pair of electrons from an electron-rich to an electron-deficient center. If you listened to Part 1 and part 2 Tetragrammaton discussion I told you how I hacked the periodic table on dimagnetic and paramagnetic atoms. Well here is another hack I did not bring up.

The chemical literature is replete with such diamagnetic processes; however, other reactions yield two-electron bonds via successive single-electron transfer (SET) reactions thus proceeding via paramagnetic intermediates. All free radicals in biology are paramagnetic. So is oxygen.

Such reactions are increasingly prominent in photochemical reactions, organic synthesis, catalysis, material synthesis, and biochemistry. Mechanisms in main-group chemistry are also dominated by two-elec- tron diamagnetic processes, and perhaps none is more common than the formation of dative-bonded adducts from the combination of an electron pair donor and an electron pair acceptor.

Such reactions led to Gilbert Lewis’s 1923 classification of donors and acceptors as bases and acids, respectively. Do your "centralized experts" do this level of homework?

**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1722655798688469124>

The family of chemical compounds known as "The Melanins" are quinones. Do you know the Q cycle in mitochondria is also dominated by quinones?

Melanin is THE MOST important biopolymeric pigment produced in a vast majority of organisms but is expertly used in mammals. Tyrosine and its hydroxylated product, dopa, form the starting material for melanin biosynthesis. Earlier studies by Raper and Mason resulted in the identification of dopachrome and dihydroxyindoles as important intermediates and paved way for the establishment of well-known Raper–Mason pathway for the biogenesis of brown to black eumelanins. Did you hear that @billgifford

This is the anabolic pathways to melanin. ### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1722656231179858164>

Tyrosinase catalyzes the oxidation of tyrosine as well as dopa to dopaquinone. Dopaquinone thus formed, undergoes intramolecular cyclization to form leucochrome, which is further oxidized to dopachrome. Dopachrome is either converted into 5,6-dihydroxyindole by decarboxylative aromatization or isomerized into 5,6-dihydroxyindole-2-carboxylic acid. Oxidative polymerization of these two dihydroxyindoles eventually produces eumelanin pigments via melanochrome. While the role of quinones in the biosynthetic pathway is very well acknowledged by centralized scientists, that of isomeric quinone methides, however, remained marginalized. ### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1709681190368657632>

BIOLOGY: In the human body, carbon dioxide is formed from the metabolism of carbohydrates, fats, and amino acids in a process known as cellular respiration, which occurs in mitochondria. While cellular respiration is notable for being a source of ATP, it also generates the waste product CO2. CO2 has a magnetic footprint in mitochondria and in the blood. NO is also liberated this way. The body gets rid of excess CO2 by breathing or sweating it out. However, CO2 in its normal range from 38 to 42 mm Hg plays various roles in the human body. It regulates the pH of blood, stimulates breathing, and influences the affinity hemoglobin has for oxygen (O2). Fluctuations in CO2 levels are highly regulated and can cause disturbances in the human body if normal levels are not maintained.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1709681584599626225>

CO2 retention is known as hypercapnia. Hypercapnia is usually due to hypoventilation or increased dead space in which the alveoli are ventilated but not perfused. In a state of hypercapnia or hypoventilation, there is an accumulation of CO2. The increased CO2 causes a drop in pH, leading to a state of respiratory acidosis. The chemoreceptor reflex is important in allowing the body to respond to changes in pO2, pCO2, NO, and pH. Chemoreceptors can be categorized as peripheral or central. Peripheral chemoreceptors are positioned in the carotid and aortic bodies. Central chemoreceptors are located near the ventrolateral surfaces of the medulla. While peripheral chemoreceptors are sensitive to changes in mostly O2 and CO2 and pH to a lesser degree, central chemoreceptors are sensitive to changes in pCO2 and pH. The glomus cells in the carotid and aortic bodies detect states of hypoxia, hypercapnia, and acidosis. On the other hand, central chemoreceptors do not detect states of hypoxia. They detect a change in PCO2 very rapidly because CO2 diffuses through the blood-brain barrier (BBB) and into the CSF easily.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1709682978471706686>

When a state of hypercapnia is introduced, central chemoreceptor activity is increased. As a result, the sympathetic outflow to the vasculature is increased, and efforts are made to increase the respiratory rate. This is what ICU physicians reported everywhere in COVID hotspots where patients were locked down or stuck in a hospital with windows that do not open. An electron used in the photoelectric effect of photosynthesis or mitochondria respiration usually encounters the three lightweight quarks in low energy collisions, but Heisenberg's uncertainity principle tells us it will occasionally encounter a rarer “molecule” of five quarks, such as an up, down and charm quark grouped on one side and an up quark and charm antiquark on the other. # Thread: The people of today would rather stand next to a man with no substance but covered in gold, rather than the man with sub
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1703914170771546225.html>
**Published:** 2023-09-09T14:04:49.000Z
**Tweets:** 10
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1703914170771546225>

The people of today would rather stand next to a man with no substance but covered in gold, rather than the man with substance whose ideas contain the wisdom of gold. They'll tell you they thirsted for wisdom, but when they hear how it comes from Nature exclusively you find that their interest in the truth was only a mirage.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1703914616705724770>

Despite the omnipresence of artificial and natural light exposure, there exists little guidance in the United States and elsewhere on light exposure in terms of timing, intensity, spectrum, and other light characteristics known to affect human health, performance, and well-being; in parallel, there is little information regarding the quantity and characteristics of light exposure that people receive.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1703914956712779944>

The modern environment, with its widespread use of electric lighting and 24/7 lifestyle, is accompanied by patterns of light and dark exposure that differ from the natural light and dark cycle (Ekirch, 2005; Wright et al., 2013) and are thought to play a role in circadian health (de la Iglesia et al., 2015; Stevens and Zhu, 2015). Less than 2 decades ago, researchers discovered the intrinsically photoreceptive retinal ganglion cells (ipRGCs) that contain the photoreceptor pigment melanopsin, thereby elucidating a direct pathway by which light influences circadian rhythms (Berson et al., 2002; Hattar et al., 2002).

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1703915275442127111>

This pathway’s myriad connections with various brain areas further expand the non–image-forming roles of light exposure (Hattar et al., 2006; Bennarroch, 2011). The classical rod and cone photoreceptors also stimulate the ipRGCs and therefore contribute to the non–image-forming effects of light (Lucas et al., 2012; Hattar et al., 2003). Increasing evidence from human and nonhuman animal studies indicates that the timing, intensity, wavelength, and duration of light exposure as well as other light characteristics may affect health.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1703915820806541624>

Research in rodents has shown that light exposure is tied to numerous physiological and behavioral outcomes, including body mass regulation (Fonken et al., 2010), metabolism (Wideman and Murphy, 2009; Coomans et al., 2013), depressive behaviors (Gonzalez and Aston-Jones, 2008; Wideman and Murphy, 2009; Bedrosian et al., 2013), and responsiveness to cancer treatment (Dauchy et al., 2014). Altering the duration of light exposure and the timing of feeding in relation to light exposure impaired glucose metabolism and resulted in weight gain for mice (Fonken et al., 2010; Coomans et al., 2013).

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1703916169571242011>

Moreover, constant light exposure in rats resulted in greater adiposity, decreased melatonin levels, changes in metabolism (increased feed efficiency and lower food and water intake), altered circadian rhythms (free running with decreased overall activity), and increased irritability compared with rats kept in constant darkness or on regular light-dark cycles (Wideman and Murphy, 2009). On the other hand, constant light deprivation for 6 weeks in rats resulted in neural damage of monoamine brain systems and depressive behavioral phenotypes compared with rats kept in 12:12 light-dark conditions (Gonzalez and Aston-Jones, 2008).

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1703916856883413299>

The wavelength of light exposure also needs to be taken into account; hamsters exposed to long-wavelength (red) light at night showed less depressive responses and less alteration of neuronal structure than hamsters exposed to shorter-wavelength (blue) light at night (Bedrosian et al., 2013). In addition, dim light exposure during rats’ normal dark period increased the growth of breast cancer tumors and conferred resistance to therapy (Dauchy et al., 2014). These animal model findings suggest that timing, duration, and additional components of light exposure are all vital constituents of a healthy environment. It is the basis of my Leptin Rx protocol.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1703917255849852992>

Similarly, human studies, both in the field and in the laboratory, add insights into the far-reaching influences of light exposure, such as on depression (Rosenthal et al., 1985; Wallace-Guy et al., 2002; Lam et al., 2016), metabolic responses (Figueiro et al., 2012; Danilenko et al., 2013; Cheung et al., 2016), body weight (Reid et al., 2014), sleep timing and quality (Wallace-Guy et al., 2002; Cho et al., 2013; Boubekri et al., 2014; Figueiro et al., 2017), sleep and behavior in Alzheimer’s disease patients (Ancoli-Israel et al., 2003; Figueiro et al., 2014, 2015), and patient recovery and well-being (Ritchie et al., 2015).

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1703917831606186322>

Several characteristics of light exposure are critical to the determination of health outcomes. Morning bright light has a well-established antidepressant effect for those suffering from seasonal affective disorder (Rosenthal et al., 1985) as well as those suffering from nonseasonal depression (Lam et al., 2016). Manipulating light exposure also resulted in alterations in metabolic function, appetite, and body fat; morning light exposure of 1300 lux for at least 45 min between 0600 h and 0900 h for 3 weeks in obese women resulted in reduced body fat and appetite (Danilenko et al., 2013).

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1703918295601029374>

END OF LESSON
