# Thread: Any time melanin sheets in your brain are degrading so is melatonin.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1635044320544952320.html>
**Published:** 2023-03-13T17:22:24.000Z
**Tweets:** 14
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1635044320544952320>

Any time melanin sheets in your brain are degrading so is melatonin. And remember Melatonin feeds back on clock genes: a theory involving the proteasome or maybe the exposome?

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1635044973262323714>

The expression of 'clock' genes occurs in all tissues, but especially in the SCN of the hypothalamus (POMc), groups of neurons in the brain that regulate circadian rhythms. Melatonin is made by RPE & gut and secreted by mitochondria in the dark in a circadian manner as… [twitter.com/i/web/status/1…](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1635044973262323714)

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1635045229790396416>

There is also considerable evidence that melatonin, in turn, acts on the SCN directly influencing the circadian 'clock' mechanisms. The most direct route by which melatonin could reach the SCN would be via the cerebrospinal fluid of the third ventricle (POMc in ependyma)

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1635045439207792640>

Melatonin could also reach the pars tuberalis (PT) of the pituitary (POMc), another melatonin-sensitive tissue, via this route.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1635045708167512064>

The major 'clock' genes include the period genes, Per1 and Per2, the cryptochrome genes, Cry1 and Cry2, the clock (circadian locomotor output cycles kaput) gene, and the Bmal1 (aryl hydrocarbon receptor nuclear translocator-like) gene. (POMc)

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1635046042092675074>

Clock/Bmal1 heterodimers act on E-box components of the promoters of Per and Cry genes to stimulate transcription. A negative feedback loop between the cryptochrome proteins & the nucleus allows the Cry and Per proteins to regulate their own transcription. A POMc self-winding… [twitter.com/i/web/status/1…](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1635046042092675074)

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1635046321248940033>

A cycle of ubiquitination and deubiquitination controls the levels of CRY protein degraded by the proteasome and, hence, the amount of protein available for feedback (POMc). Thus, it provides a post-translational component to the circadian clock mechanism. [@hubermanlab](https://twitter.com/hubermanlab)

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1635047388384751617>

BMAL1 also stimulates transcription of REV-ERBα and, in turn, is also partially regulated by negative feedback by REV-ERBα = heme chromophore.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1635047676591980544>

In the 'black widow' model of transcription, proteasomes destroy transcription factors that are needed only for a particular period of time. In the model proposed herein, the interaction of melatonin & the proteasome is required to adjust the SCN clock to changes in the… [twitter.com/i/web/status/1…](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1635047676591980544)

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1635048133838376962>

In particular, we predict that melatonin inhibition of the proteasome interferes with negative feedback loops (CRY/PER and REV-ERBα) on Bmal1 transcription genes in both the SCN and PT.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1635048481084538880>

Melatonin inhibition of the proteasome also stabilize BMAL1 protein itself in the SCN, particularly at night when melatonin is naturally elevated. Melatonin inhibition of the proteasome could account for the effects of melatonin on circadian rhythms associated w/ molecular… [twitter.com/i/web/status/1…](https://twitter.com/i/web/status/1635048481084538880)

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1635048668226281473>

The interaction of melatonin with the proteasome in the hypothalamus also provides a model for explaining the dramatic 'time-of-day' effect of melatonin injections on the reproductive status of seasonal breeders.

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1635049062968983552>

This also explains why modern humans are infertile when they are in need of melanin renovations in their bodies due to a lack of all the MSH from a lack of sun.

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1635049198390497280>

Finally, the model predicts that a proteasome inhibitor such as bortezomib would modify circadian rhythms in a manner similar to melatonin.

[ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25369242](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/25369242)

# Thread: What is missing here? A lot if you're a Black swan mitochondriac.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1634660123456012291.html>
**Published:** 2023-03-13T17:22:24.000Z
**Tweets:** 27
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634660123456012291>

What is missing here? A lot if you're a Black swan mitochondriac. Let's do a lesson today. When we speak about mitochondria, where water is created from food, realize they are loaded with electrons and protons we are engaging the physics of the so-called sub-atomic world

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634660354469818368>

Therefore, we need to understand how size links to thermodynamics. This is why the physics of elementary particles and their interactions with light is important to a mitochondriac. When things shrink people understanding tends to shrink as well.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634660636415205376>

The first thing to understand when the scale shrinks in nature the strength of the electromagnetic force increases. Remember the force carrier for this force is the PHOTON.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634661853560225794>

QM confounds because the "small world" of the chloroplast/mitochondria dont conform to the world we observe. Photons precede the creation of the DC electric current in cells. Just understaning biochemicals in pathways tell ya' nothing without knowing what phontons are doing

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634662042442379264>

The electromagnetic force is much stronger at small scales than it is at larger scales.
To illustrate this point, consider the size of an atomic nucleus in a WBG semiconductor is approximately 100,000 times less than the size of a water molecule.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634662145165074433>

Now understand, a water molecule is smaller than the visible light wavelength in a photon by a factor of 1000. So even using our best optical microscopes we couldn’t hope to see or observe a molecule of water, let alone an atomic nucleus.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634662256980946954>

Thus there is an obvious problem of even adequately visualizing the objects in question that life deals with. It should be no wonder why centralized research/medicine is blind to what a mitochondriac understands.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634662657213997057>

When light is in motion, just changing the electromagnetic field in space won’t bend light at all. Physics has proved this; a constant electric or magnetic field won't change its behavior at all. Water will cause light to change its bending.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634662915612581888>

The reason for this is tied to Maxwell’s classical equations on electromagnetic waves. It’s like rising the water level and expecting the waves on the surface to deflect.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634663618250768385>

But here is where things get interesting for biology: changing the viscosity of the liquid at a certain time interval (day & night) will deflect the wave on the surface. Can a mitochondrion change its viscosity by altering the chemistry inside its matrix where its made?

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634663860081655808>

Yes, they can. Now consider hydrogen bonding networks in H20 are very dynamic. So dynamic that we cannot perceive how fast they adapt. The work at atto or femtoseconds when light photons interact with them.

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634664102244106242>

The fast adaptive behavior of these H+ networks in water, alone determines the dielectric constant of the medium they are within. The dielectric constant determines the refractive index of water. Normal bulk water has a high dielectric value (78).

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634664660703096832>

Things with high dielectric constants tend to be highly polar and water is. Because of this polarity just about anything can be dissolved in water and made useful in some way. Cells use this to build WBG semiconductors from collagen biomolecules in us. Water surrounds all

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634664868002295810>

Low pH aqueous environments (inflammation) have excessive protons and a lowered dielectric value. This means they do something to light inside our tissues. Alkaline pH also changes the dielectric constant.

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634665169539276802>

When you learn that all living cells emit light as Dr. Huberman just did this changes the game of how you need to understand and study water. He is in the process of doing just that now after our meeting.

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634665526575214593>

The other chemicals in the matrix, like exotic atoms used in WBG semiconduction in cells = Ca, Mg, Na, Cl, I, K etc....can also alter the dielectric values in the mitochondrial matrix. O2 does too. This changes light in cells.

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634665815860551682>

Each photon of light emitted has a frequency and that frequency has very specific, sensitive, and precise jobs to do. Cell specialize in the photons they create. They only create light in the UV spectrum.

### Tweet 18
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634666725865058304>

Why did cells choose this? Because UV light is the only part of the visible spectrum of light that can undergo non-linear optical transformation. This is where you are leaving thermodynamic levels and touching the quantum level of processing in cells.

### Tweet 19
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634667096381505539>

The water podcast above only discusses water in the classical world. That is important to understand but in no way does it explain life, or what cells are doing below the cell level with light they are pumping into our system to build complexity from chaos, and order from it

### Tweet 20
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634667825339027459>

When light alters H20 its dielectric value (78-160) guess what changes the most? Viscosity changes the most in cells when this happens. The inside of your mitochondria is a gel-like piezoelectric flexoelectric quasicrystal. Only quantum mechanics can tell its secrets.

### Tweet 21
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634668600979070976>

This changes the quality of light emission from the semiconductors inside of us and how they work with water. It changes how electrons & protons move within our atomic lattice everywhere including mitochondria.

### Tweet 22
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634669031922835461>

This changes how they process energy and information that send to your nucleus where DNA is. DNA is an antenna for the light carried in cell water. The smallest things in you make the largest difference in decentralized medicine & health.

### Tweet 23
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634669363755208706>

For example, I shared w/ [@hubermanlab](https://twitter.com/hubermanlab) that our ATPase @ 9000 revolutions transfers 90,000 protons per minute in our cells. Any change in a dielectric value directly alters the electromagnetic force generation capable between things in the water. Before me, he never knew this

### Tweet 24

He is in the process of assimilating this new info so I am sure that this information will change the nonlinear optics in his brain by altering the interaction of his melanin sheets using VUV-IR light to change the dielectric constant in his neurons to think anew.

### Tweet 25

The human ATPase is a quantum machine that has to be surrounded by H20 with a specific isotope of hydrogen in the water running it. It revolves with 100% efficiency using IR-A light from the sun! This is why IR-A penetrates deeply to get to your colony of stowaway bacteria

Sunlight changes the viscoeleastic tensions in mitochondria. Water becomes a perfect aperiodic crystal that stores energy and creates memory and becomes coherent = consciousness = This changes the size & shape changes of the mitochondria = size /shape change=thermodynamics

# Thread: TODAY'S quantum biology lesson: WHAT IS FOOD TO YOUR EXPERTS? Food is made from sunlight that has slowed down by chlorop
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1634189072729006080.html>
**Published:** 2023-03-11T20:58:38.000Z
**Tweets:** 27
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634189072729006080>

TODAY'S quantum biology lesson: WHAT IS FOOD TO YOUR EXPERTS? Food is made from sunlight that has slowed down by chlorophyll. Light energy is transformed into biomolecules that create chemical energy.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634189698909323264>

Do you know why most people just focus on food macros? Because it's easy to explain. It's not because it's correct.
How much do you know about photosynthesis?
Where does the 02 in photosynthesis originate from? Do you know all food webs come from this one reaction on Earth?

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634190579499147266>

The oxygen released during photosynthesis comes from the splitting of water during the light-dependent reaction. The first step in photosynthesis is to split water into positive & negative charges.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634190813138759680>

Remember, the electrons lost from the reaction center in photosystem II must be replaced. The splitting of water serves to replace these lost electrons.
Explain the energy flow and conversion of photosynthesis: chloroplasts "capture" sunlight energy in two ways.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634191789195239425>

Do you know what the ionization energy is to charge separate water? It is 12.06 eV. That means a chemistry book says we need soft Xrays to do what the chemical reaction requires in a leaf. Do you believe that? If not, how does nature do it? Nature never makes mistakes.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634192110793506818>

During Day 2 with [@hubermanlab](https://twitter.com/hubermanlab) and [@RickRubin](https://twitter.com/RickRubin) I began the discussion about water with this simple fact. From there I discussed what really is going on between light water and chlorophyll.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634192598851018752>

Chlorophyll was the single most important chemical on Earth for millions of years because it was the chemical that sculpted food and our atmosphere to create the stage for all life that came after its innovation. What it does is the key to understanding ALL DIETS.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634193026992988162>

Light ''excites'' electrons in "pigment molecules" (chlorophyll), and light provides the energy to split water molecules, providing more electrons as well as hydrogen ions. H+ are then sorted isotopically by UV light.
Light Energy is transformed into electroChemical Energy

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634193381893996545>

Pigment molecules = a doped wide band semiconductor that uses Mg and N to do the job. The choice of atoms has a lot to do with the first ionization energy of 12.06 eV found in water.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634193825500471296>

Solar energy contains the power to be transformed into many things including heat. Light below the UV spectrum crashes into atoms in chlorophyll and is transformed. That collision creates foodstuffs. Food is "a form of matter built by light complexity"

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634194061765615617>

Did you know Joseph Fourier made an important contribution to physics in the nineteenth century when he published his mathematical formulation of the manner in which heat propagates?

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634194267718467586>

Today, Fourier’s law tells us that heat flows from regions of higher temperature to regions of lower: “that heat flux is proportional to the difference in temperature.” This law is a remarkable insight into universality built into nature.

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634194650524164096>

Here, we have many disparate macroscopic systems, from solids to gasses to liquids, each containing an inordinate sum of molecules, each conforming to a law of heat conductivity. To a chemist, Ilya Prigogine, Fourier’s description was the “birth of the science of complexity”

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634195172304011265>

Food is "a light complexity". Food is a dissipative system in nature built to transmit solar energy to mitochondria to build an ideal hydrogen-based Carnot engine for complex life.
Food is one form of stored solar energy for life. Do you understand food in this way?

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634195989253718017>

Food gurus certainly don't.
The key phenomenon of nature is how light energy is transformed. It can become mechanical, chemical, or vital energy (DC electric current) Light energy undergoes a continual conversion of itself as it interacts with matter.

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634196200772493313>

The atomic organization of matter in cells is the key to energy transformation and the criticality that life needs to operate.
Back to how the food gurus learn about food: biochemistry.

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634197031001391106>

Describe how ATP is produced during the light-capturing reactions of photosynthesis.
Photo-phosphorylation is how it is done. Do you know what P is really up to in us? Might it have something to do with that 12.06 eV problem?

### Tweet 18
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634197210089897984>

Do you understand the purpose of the Calvin cycle and how it relates to photosynthesis? The domain of the Calvin cycle in photosynthesis is the light-independent portion of the photosynthetic process.

### Tweet 19
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634197435764420608>

ATP from photosystem II provides energy and NADPH from PS I provides a source of hydrogens and high-energy electrons needed to bind the hydrogen to carbon. This is where biochem stops its understanding. Biophysics teaches us that hydrogens must be in its protium isotopic form

### Tweet 20
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634197673094836224>

NOTHING ELSE MATTERS.
UV light makes sure the deuterium content in food is quantized. This is why chlorophylls do not use UV light to create food. Check chlorophyll's absorption spectra if you do not believe me.

### Tweet 21
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634199381833392130>

UV light is used to control deuterium levels in foods not transform solar energy into food. This biophysical reality is not present in biochem textbooks. It was presented in my Vermont 2018 talk. I think it flew right over most heads back then.

### Tweet 22
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634199476108705793>

Back to centralized biochemical beliefs: In the Calvin Cycle, three molecules each containing 5 carbons (Ribulose bisphosphate) are joined to 3 carbon dioxide molecules to produce 6 molecules of 3-phosphoglycerate.

### Tweet 23
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634199572300931072>

This reaction is catalyzed by the enzyme rubisco. A series of reactions consume 6 ATP and 6 NADPH to produce 6 glyceraldehyde 3-phosphate molecules.

### Tweet 24
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634199755256479746>

One of these is used ultimately to produce glucose, and the other five are turned back into the original ribulose bisphosphate at a cost of a further 3 ATP molecules. That is how biochemistry sees it. They have no idea how light is stored at the electronic level in foods

### Tweet 25
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634200044046893065>

Because of that belief they have no earthly idea of what mitochondria are doing to foods at the subatomic level either. That entire process is controlled by the thermodynamics of a Carnot heat engine.

### Tweet 26
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634200099805954051>

Biochemistry is a half-truth about what food really is. Food is an electromagnetic bar code of sunlight. That is the biophysical reality. Never settle for less information than you deserve to know from anyone.

### Tweet 27
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1634200405906186241>

Listen, watch, and learn. When you know better you do better. If you want more lessons from a decentralized physician join me at [patreon.com/DrJackKruse](http://www.patreon.com/DrJackKruse)

Follow the yellow brick road of the retina. When the choroid gets thick humans get fat and the risk of neurodegeneration rises huge!

### Tweet 6

And now we know why myopia is so common.....

### Tweet 7

The brighter the blue light the worse the vision because the photoreceptors are also destroyed = why Dave Asprey is an idiot.

### Tweet 8

Because of how chromophores work the color of indoor environments could also make you sick = Yarkovsky effect.

### Tweet 9

Blue light is a neurotoxin in the right dose and when IR-A and UV light is missing. Blue light in AM sunlight is a good thing. Light is something not to fuck around with but humans are too smart for their own good. They created their KT event in 1893

# Thread: The butterfly effect from my weekend.....@hubermanlab wants to come and visit me in my lab and do a podcast mano y mano
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1632813281164312581.html>
**Published:** 2023-03-07T13:18:41.000Z
**Tweets:** 17
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632813281164312581>

The butterfly effect from my weekend.....[@hubermanlab](https://twitter.com/hubermanlab) wants to come and visit me in my lab and do a podcast mano y mano with me. In "light" of this I want Andrew to be aware of the butterfly effects of what I said to him so he is ready for the next round of inquiry on light

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632813674577506307>

CLIP = Corticotropin-like intermediate lobe peptide and blue light without the protection of IR-A and UV light in the eye cause ACTH release from cells with POMC and as ACTH rises so does CLIP.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632814130221527042>

Implications? In humans, this is how blue light raises blood glucose and insulin. CLIP is the major cause of diabetes in modern man. A big statement backed up by the pic below.
CLIP raises insulin in response to blue light exposure without any food exposed to the organism.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632814354641956866>

CLIP raises insulin in response to blue light exposure without any food exposed to the organism. This peptide has massive effects on the exocrine pancreas. CLIP's physiological role has been investigated in various tissues specifically in the central nervous system.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632814860982427649>

Big Pharma has been trying to keep a lid on this for a long time. So beware of where our discussion will go. What cells specifically make POMC in humans?

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632819563975909382>

Andrew based on our 10 hours of discussion on light/water/magnetism if you look at that list and remember what I told you about in 1923 about mitogenic radiation and NaCl in the CSF you might begin to understand these slides below with new eyes

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632819726811385856>

Remember Gurwitsch's work on mitosis here.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632820532788731904>

When you look at this and see "mitosis" and death and you realize those are migrating cells from within that only migrated to the surface from the skull to get to the UV light and when they got there then mitosis showed up on the surface = "neuroectoderm psy ops" .

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632821200832323585>

Now for the bow on the present [@hubermanlab](https://twitter.com/hubermanlab) . Thyrotropic-releasing hormone (TRH) released from neurons in the paraventricular nucleus of the hypothalamus is stimulated directly by the hormone, leptin.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632821648356257792>

Leptin increases melanocortin (α-MSH)\] and it is required for TRH expression! This means people with hypothyroidism cannot make POMC! And this makes their tissues atrophic for sunlight = why so many humans have sun hypersensitivity. Think Lupus.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632821810273058816>

The melanocortin system activates the TRH promoter on DNA through the phosphorylation of the signal transducer and activator of transcription (Stat3). The Stat response element in the TRH promoter is required for the effects of leptin = Leptin-melanocortin tract wisdom

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632822119288512515>

What does this mean for our species? POMC is a prohormone that gives rise to several biologically active peptides that are expressed primarily in the pituitary and brain. This isn’t limited to those tissues.

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632822354094030851>

Sunlight ONLY in the visible spectra stimulates POMC with help of the TSH. Humans who live under fake light do not make POMC and this means mitosis is defective in their cells = onion experiment [@hubermanlab](https://twitter.com/hubermanlab)

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632823971069542400>

When mitosis is defective in mammals their cells do not divide and they become mobile and invade other tissues. Now I brought you to the next blog......QE #30 and why this slide has the cancer word on it. Centralized medicine has a sacred cow=$$$

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632824492220116994>

No POMC = lots of cancer, autism, autoimmunity, and diabetes. With a slide plate of heart disease and a ton of neuro degeneration. WHY? Where is melanopsin? In your brain, vessels and fatmass.

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632824839588200450>

OK......I'll stop now because your head is spinning but trust me......I am just warming up [@hubermanlab](https://twitter.com/hubermanlab)

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632826199956135937>

What does this all mean? [@hubermanlab](https://twitter.com/hubermanlab) This next post is for you and you alone. You will know what it means now. What do cephalopods and theropod dinosaurs have in common?

# Thread: Your dreams don't make you great, your work ethic that grows them does.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1632732472638341127.html>
**Published:** 2023-03-07T13:18:41.000Z
**Tweets:** 10
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632732472638341127>

Your dreams don't make you great, your work ethic that grows them does. Dreams are free but the journey is not. When we roll up our sleeves, pitch in, and work hard together, united for a common cause, there is no telling how successful we can be.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632732738330710016>

Teamwork will make our dreams work. Why do I think work is the best 4 letter word? We should work for just the pleasure we find in work, the satisfaction there is in developing things, in creating something new. Also, the association's business begets.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632733164832694272>

The person who does not work for the love of work, but only for money, is not likely to make money nor to find much fun in life. Today think about making your vocation your vacation in how you perceive it.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632733432357822468>

Natural harmony requires seasonal destruction and renewal. Without renewal, we cannot sustain. We have to come apart so we don't fall apart. The sun renews plants, they renew us, we renew them, and night renews day.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632733768267206656>

Each season renews different aspects of life. Water is the scent of our renewal. Focus your intention on what you're building, not whats already fallen away.

Are you daringly authentic in your life?

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632733987268435968>

My friend and tribe member, Rick Rubin poses this question in his new masterpiece book, The Creative Act: A Way of Being. None of us are perfect but we all can be authentic.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632734253497700352>

Anybody can talk, but walking the talk, now that takes real courage and a real commitment to be authentic to have integrity, and be and act as you said you would, regardless of who is around you. There is nothing more authentic than seeing a person being themselves.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632734711393943556>

I laid all my cards on the table this weekend in Malibu and I think the world will see it. Imagine going through your day being unapologetically you, I just did it for 4 days here. Rick improved the fidelity of my signal.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632735071827382272>

Every night before I sleep I am ready to run a race in my mind &that race is one that I look forward to every night. I don't race against others but I race against myself. No one makes me work harder than my mind.

I'm ready for what the outcome of this weekend brings to me

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632735876697702400>

Thanks to [@RickRubin](https://twitter.com/RickRubin) and [@hubermanlab](https://twitter.com/hubermanlab) for a weekend to share ideas slay some sacred cows and become an agent provocateur for the world. We can change the world with our thoughts.

# Thread: A.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1632444311223812096.html>
**Published:** 2023-03-07T13:18:41.000Z
**Tweets:** 22
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632444311223812096>

A. Decentralized networks are based on a mastery of time. Darwin lived in a world where the church made everyone believe for 2000 years that the Earth was no older than 6000 years old.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632444518896394240>

B. Lyell’s observations in geology had a tremendous impact on how time would affect Darwin before he got on “The Beagle”. It made Darwin realize just how important timing was to biology. How he viewed time was the key to his theory

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632444659141312514>

C. Today, it remains noteworthy that it is exactly this timing principle, that is foundational to the cumulative power of incremental change over millions and billions of years and thousands of generations. Darwin realized time was critical to life’s formation.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632444828675117056>

D. Both of his ideas, natural selection, and conditions of existence, are subject to a deep understanding of true space-time in a palindromic fashion.
Darwin knew it, but he could not figure out how both worked and it almost sunk his theory back in his time.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632445079561584640>

E. His failures stimulated me to find the real truth in what controls time and what sculpts cells.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632445805394288640>

F. They're at the heart of his evolutionary opus if you read it. Just like an individual basalt flow, an asteroid strike, or the tiny amount of sedimentary rock, that can be carried away annually by a waterway, the change within species due to natural selection is quite small

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632446459835736064>

G. If we just consider it over its life span of some years or decades. Yet, Darwin was able to grasp how far it could take life when given the “proper geological time” to work with.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632446989890879488>

H. That time was able to apply consistent pressure for a few tens or hundreds of thousands of years, and those small changes start to begin to add up to massive alterations to the organism.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632447541253136386>

I. Ironically, I am pointing out the very same problem that modern chemistry and biology have today, because of how they fail to include molecular timing as a vital ingredient in the recipe of how life forms and evolves from the chaos the world finds itself in presently

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632447854982864896>

J. How does modern life affect the evolutionary flux right now? The answer is called transgenerational epigenetics. Light is the lever. This is our new name for “conditions of existence”.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632448247699734528>

K. Today, modern science has no idea that the smallest changes in “molecular timing” in cells can cause massive changes in the cellular response in one hundred years to cause massive neolithic disease. POMC is WBCs who lose their VUV signal emission = MI risk

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632448638378196993>

L. My ideas & Darwin’s ideas are cut from the very same QUILT because they are both based upon time. Darwin used “big time”, and I am using “quantum time” to figure out the riddle. Einstein’s science is based on the Theory of Relativity & also uses time as a key variable.

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632448773057290240>

M. The analogy is startling when you consider it. Modern science has lost the perspective of nanoscopic time on the biochemistry of life because it works on quantum coherence and not in the world of classical physics that modern science sees today.

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632449029698383872>

N. The bioenergetics of ATP hydrolysis can not explain real-time enzyme fluxes and kinetics when they are measured in living tissues. I can, using quantum field theory. To see how blind biology really is, they gave Mitchell a Noble prize for this in 1978.

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632449196216426497>

O. I do not expect them to adapt fast. Embarrassment is hard to overcome in short order. Every organic chemist on this planet believes Mitchell is right about membranes and chemiosmosis and if you continue to follow those organic chemists, you will die faster than you should.

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632449287299940353>

P. A bold statement, I know. But as I said before, I believe I have the goods. Once I realized quantum time was the etiology of our demise, I sensed that nothing so makes us desire to spend our time wisely than to reflect on vita brevis; the brevity of life.

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632449441096699904>

Q. Bad timing is ironically why Darwin, could never figure out the epigenetic riddle of his condition of existence. Einstein was not born in 1868 when Darwin wrote his masterpiece. He had no idea time was relative to everything.

### Tweet 18
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632449588480311296>

R. Traditionally, classically trained scientists have held that low-level electromagnetic fields couldn’t be harmful because they weren’t potent enough to break chemical bonds in a living organism. This one belief has led us to the precipice of disaster.

### Tweet 19
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632449661427679232>

S. Low-level electromagnetic radiation is the spark of life of evolution and it is our death star when it is misused. The Russians have known this since the 1950s in my opinion and they have been using space as their laboratory to study it closely.

### Tweet 20
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632449991209009154>

T. When you look at epidemiologic studies, however, the stamp of bad quantum time & rapid de-evolutionary evidence is present in every form of life today & seen in our oceans our climate, and even our clinics daily.

### Tweet 21
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632450236919734272>

U. If the brain can not sense it, it does not believe it can exist or affect life. This keeps it hidden from us all. Humans must explore the concept of circadian time more to find their cures.

### Tweet 22
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632450419023826944>

V. I hope you liked this walk back in space-time. END

# Thread: What do you value? I value consistency.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1633094822515933185.html>
**Published:** 2023-03-06T19:32:27.000Z
**Tweets:** 5
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1633094822515933185>

What do you value? I value consistency. I value it because nature has taught me her atomic precision is required every day in rearranging the atoms in my cells that I moved around yesterday in my process of living.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1633095142671368193>

Our cells are wide-band gapped semiconductor factories that require precision manufacturing and the sun and our circadian mechanism is how cells perform their version of photolithography to keep us running at high efficiency.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1633095522167791618>

To be diligent is to truly focus, pay attention to details, follow through, the surroundings, and the people affected, and really put into action your heart and soul to accomplish great things.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1633095710722719746>

Health is the natural consequence of consistently applying the basic fundamentals of nature that are built into your cells.

We will not and can not find peace of mind if we are afraid of the hurricanes of modern living.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1633095948384575488>

Through our daily actions, we reveal our deepest beliefs. This is one of my deepest beliefs; try to find your deepest belief & ask yourself, why? Then finish your thought experiment by answering that question for yourself. When you emerge, you will be transformed.

# Thread: Part two of the podcast begins in 5 hours.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1632380344916938752.html>
**Published:** 2023-03-06T19:32:27.000Z
**Tweets:** 30
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632380344916938752>

Part two of the podcast begins in 5 hours. The discussion begins with water. We will start the story with the 1901 Nobel Prize with Roentgen. He was my first water muse. [@hubermanlab](https://twitter.com/hubermanlab) [@RickRubin](https://twitter.com/RickRubin)

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632381025945899010>

Roentgen is known for his discovery of X-rays but what he found using Xrays with water still has yet to be unlocked by centralized biology. The 20-21 st physicists have slowly unlocked what Roentgen observed in 1901 to validate his observation

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632382446049296390>

In 1913 a book The Fitness of the Environment was written by Henderson a scientist/theologian who said the strangeness of water makes it fit for life on Earth and in the Universe. The queerness of water is explained by part one of the podcast = wideband semiconduction.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632383076436414464>

Marshal MacLuhan said the medium is the message. This is buried in Shannon's information theory but the story of life is that water is the medium, means, and message that life needs to exist.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632383839216762882>

Water is strangely fit for life because it is the most polar molecule in the chemistry and biology possible on Earth. This means it is easily polarized by an electric field.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632384147477139456>

As I said yesterday in the Rubin podcast daytime fills the ionosphere with electric fields that disappear at night when magnetic fields dominate the ionosphere. This is how life innovated wakefulness from the primordial state of sleep on Earth.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632384540802166787>

Liquid water has a high dielectric constant because of its polar ability. This is also why it can dissolve almost anything in chemistry. But its dielectric constant can change when certain things are done to it in a cell.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632385096098680833>

The father of modern biochemistry Albert Szent Gyorgyi gave a lecture in 1941 that changed my life when I read the transcript as a young resident in neurosurgery on bone physiology.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632385445916200962>

Here he posited briefly that the electronic state of proteins and water appeared to him to look like a semiconductor that was described by Bose in 1903 before Marconi stole his work.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632386030090457090>

This idea immediately got my brain looking at this below to figure out the puzzle of water below the cell level where Szent Gyorgyi told future scientists/clinicians to look.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632386767440744448>

It leads me to the work of Martin Chaplin who is currently the best water researcher alive. From his work I found Emilio Del Guidice and Preparta who were nuclear physicists working on cold fusion and wound up studying the nuclear effects in water.

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632387342861475840>

This lead me to Mae Wan Ho. In Ho's work I was reconnected to another giant I found earlier. Glibert Ling. Ling was responsible for showing me Nobel Prizes were often given to scientists who gave the world half truths. I talked about that in part one of the Rubin podcast

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632387658159902726>

Without Ling there would be no MRI machine for neurosurgery and the MRI machine was built because of the queerness of the protons in water and how they react to RF radiations. This is the domain of my profession since 1991.

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632388020237406215>

One cannot understand biology without understanding the physics of water inside a cell. All of these things will be detailed today.

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632388342624174080>

I found Gerry Pollacks work much later who basically took many of the idea of water researchers mentioned above and decided to test their ideas with simple experiments in his lab in Univ Washington.

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632388887816593408>

Pollack wrote a book about it that made the dense science of the physics of water accessible to a third-grade education. His book is a masterpiece on explaining water easily to the masses but this book will not satisfy rigorous scientists in biochemists and neurobiology

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632389119031787520>

Chaplin. Del Guidice, and Preparta went uber deep to the quantum level where the electronic state of water can be understood via the Lamb Shift.

### Tweet 18
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632389770134552576>

Roentgen gave us the idea of the two-state theory of water in 1901 but Wilse Robinson at Texas Tech reintroduced it to centralized science right when I had my epiphany at the foot of David.

### Tweet 19
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632390241674989568>

Chaplin took what we knew about the hydrogen bonding network of ice and liquid water and said that when water dielectric constant was shifted water became more ordered from a tetra hedron crystal to a icsahedral crystal and that shape & size explained the queerness of water

### Tweet 20
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632390559724883969>

Low density water (ice) can change to high density water without breaking any bonds. The bonds just had to vary by some mechanism. Light is that mechanism.

### Tweet 21
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632391114887147520>

This brought me to the work of Nobel Laureates Ilya Prigogine, Penrose, and Shechtman on aperiodic crystals and dissipative structures. I was fast becoming a quantum clinician in my quest to solve the riddle below. Water was at the apex of the enigma

### Tweet 22
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632391482756960257>

As a neurosurgeon the odd thing about the brain is the fluid-filled sac it sits in and the holes in the brain filled with this fluid that was made mostly from water but had a very unusual chemical constitution when you look it from the periodic table lens

### Tweet 23
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632391789947781120>

The brain is also odd in how blood comes to it compared to all other organs. It enters from outside its substance and then pierces it in the subarachnoid space to get to neurons. I wondered about this and water and why the relationship is unique.

### Tweet 24
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632392861181435905>

Chaplin's work is something that will please [@RickRubin](https://twitter.com/RickRubin) because he linked water to a quasicrystalline structure and in this structure, Cosmologist Penrose and Isreal's Shechtman found that the golden ratio was buried in golden ratio number 1.611803398874989. This stunned me

### Tweet 25
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632393339248209921>

[@hubermanlab](https://twitter.com/hubermanlab) maybe shocked to learn Stanford confirmed the two state model of water by using Roentgen Xrays in scattering experiments. Water comes in two forms: LDW and HDW. LDW = ice and HDW = liquid water.

### Tweet 26
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632394623703486465>

I realized in the human brain's CSF HDW water becomes a different animal because of its chemistry & this explained the Leptin Rx in detail to me @ the foot of David. CSF is created from blood at 0.3ml per hour=450cc/day= CSF is turned over 3 times a day. I asked myself why?

### Tweet 27
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632395402413748224>

CSF has a specific gravity of 1.007 and a pH of 7.33-7.35 in health. It varies dramatically in disease. 95% of CSF is made by the choroid plexus in the 4th ventricle and where iodine is added to it. I asked myself why looking at this below

### Tweet 28
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632396009455374338>

Then I looked at this and it all clicked.

### Tweet 29
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632396523135959040>

I found what water was doing and why Ling’s work linked to the creation of the MRI machine and why Peter Mitchell is a biochemical fraud Laureate holding a Nobel Prize.

### Tweet 30
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1632397023172513792>

The END. The rest will be illuminated today in 3 hours from now with [@hubermanlab](https://twitter.com/hubermanlab) and [@RickRubin](https://twitter.com/RickRubin)

# Thread: I asked myself this question below while I watched the sunset thinking about my upcoming podcast with Huberman.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1631138980665208833.html>
**Published:** 2023-02-27T14:05:31.000Z
**Tweets:** 17
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631138980665208833>

I asked myself this question below while I watched the sunset thinking about my upcoming podcast with Huberman. Successful people sense their purpose and destiny to help others. The purpose of our destiny is to give today its motivation.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631139320181432320>

Today's decisions, choices, and determination should play a starring role in creating our future. Our heart knows the geography of our destiny. Don't allow your mind to ruin that path. Use it as your beacon.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631139416658812929>

One thing is for sure today, you are destined to become the person you decide to be by using new actions. You can never become that new person who defaults to doing the same old things.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631140569790464001>

An easy life is rarely meaningful and meaningful life is rarely easy. Thriving is fighting. Surviving is barely getting by tomorrow. Why do so many settle for a D when a C, B, or A is possible?

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631141164760924160>

Sometimes we need a little crisis to get our adrenaline flowing and help us realize our potential. Potential comes with the entanglement, coherence, and harmony that nature provides. For example, Take a look at the plants.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631142004787666944>

Some wipe their asses with it. Why do people not understand why technology is killing us? Is it as simple as saying the more technology we use the less oxygen our cells can process? The answer is yes. Technology use breeds hypoxia in a human mitochondrion.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631142400591646720>

Some come together and thrive peacefully in our gardens and parks under the direction of sunlight. They lean on each other and share water falling from the clouds and minerals in the soil without trying to outdo one another.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631142889756536832>

They serve as an example that we should see beyond differences & embrace each other in love while allowing our uniqueness to add color to our world. If plants can live in harmony and show their beauty to the world, humans should be able to do more to each other than we have

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631143509620150272>

The man who lies to himself & listens to his own lie comes to a point where he can't distinguish the truth within him, or around him, and so loses all respect for himself and for others.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631143895244349441>

The truth is rarely pure & never simple. The ultimate test of your knowledge is your capacity to transfer it to another.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631144206847623168>

The modern world is a poor seedbed for clear thinking because of the collateral effects of tech abuse. Those of us who live in towns and cities are bombarded on a daily basis with information that seeks to warp the value judgments we make.

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631144860219482112>

The whole point of technology marketing as "human progress" is to make us irrational and dumb us down to control us. It is that simple. That truth is tough for most to swallow.

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631145529613725698>

When we discover who we are, the cage door is open. When we walk in that light connected to nature we become free. The main journey in life is the one within.

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631145796627312642>

Successful people sense their purpose and destiny to help others. The purpose of our destiny is to give today its motivation. Today's decisions and determination play a starring role in creating our future.

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631145930543058945>

Your heart knows the geography of your destiny. Use it, and not your friends or family, as your beacon. One thing is for sure today, you are destined to become the person you decide to be.

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631146210651152384>

Live more consciously today. Stop sleepwalking through your life. Your life is something that should be experienced and not coasted through. Today begin to discover your uniqueness..... then discipline yourself to develop it.

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1631146888601780224>

You'll discover with time that learning something new, no matter how complex the topic, isn't hard when you have a reason to want to know it. That's it for now. Night.

# Thread: Birds are modern dinosaurs and this explains why testicular melanization is more frequently observed during the breeding
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1630043478188081153.html>
**Published:** 2023-02-27T14:05:31.000Z
**Tweets:** 12
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630043478188081153>

Birds are modern dinosaurs and this explains why testicular melanization is more frequently observed during the breeding season of birds. Decentralized clinicians know why. Centralized ones are perplexed.
[pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21306466/](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21306466/)

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630046053465456641>

A big clue to the mystery is that most birds breed in spring, while Antarctic birds like Emperor penguins breed annually during the antarctic winter, June through August.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630046587123646464>

More clues: The earliest known mammals were the morganucodontids, tiny shrew-size creatures that lived in the shadows of the dinosaurs 210 million years ago. They were small hairy and stayed underground because they were obvious prey animals to the therapod dinosaur eyes

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630052932044324869>

In the domain Eukaryota there are more than 100000 naturally occurring hemoglobin variants with single amino acid substitutions throughout the molecule that have been discovered. Most are related to aromatic amino acids = clue

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630054508888727552>

Melanin and hemoglobin are central to these clues. All mammals and marsupials like the platypus and wombat have also been found to glow under ultraviolet (UV) light=big clue. Dinosaurs would have seen them easily

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630055172608073730>

This explains why these animals made it through the last extinction event. Bigger clue. Research has shown there was an intriguing evolutionary shift in the type of melanin used between cold-blooded and warm-blooded species found on Earth.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630056309625085953>

Animals that survived the last extinction event only use atoms from H to I on the periodic table. That's atomic numbers 1- 53. Between atomic [#s](/hashtag/s), 1-53 many atoms aren't used while the ones that are used are often amplified. This fact should get the mitochondriac curious.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630057071742705664>

Why would cells on the Earth do this? When humans have severe mitochondrial failure in their highest capacity organs giving the salts helps and flies in the face of conventional wisdom.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630060000050642947>

when heart failure occurs the heart loses its magnetic flux based on MEG data. Iron's oxidation state plays an important role in regulating cell death during stress conditions by a process called ferroptosis

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630061312179937281>

Abnormal increases in Fe2+ in lysosomes and endoplasmic reticulum (ER) promote ferroptosis and melanin destruction in mammals and cause ELF-UV to rise, infertility to rise, and a loss of neurologic problems with skin diseases. Last clue. Sunscreens do the same.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630064275141140482>

I will unfold this story with Rick Rubin because he used a technique to optimize himself against acoustic loss in his brain. It also explains why the most common criticism of AC/DC is that their songs are excessively simple and formulaic.

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630064692893720577>

It also explains why on Metallica's musical tracks the drum beats of Ulrich are ahead of the guitars and the drum beats of Phil Rudd are behind the guitar tracks on AC/DC songs. Last clue!

# Thread: The human cochlea uses water in its spiral design and melanin in the organ of Corti to use sound waves to create an elec
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1629609446325329920.html>
**Published:** 2023-02-27T14:05:31.000Z
**Tweets:** 5
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1629609446325329920>

The human cochlea uses water in its spiral design and melanin in the organ of Corti to use sound waves to create an electric current for the brain to decipher using semiconduction.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1629609773296504838>

Sound vibrations can become electrical energy through the principle of electromagnetic induction. Electromagnetic induction generates electrical current when using a magnetic field. Mitochondria generate that field with its F0 spinning head

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1629610392002371586>

When a magnetic field and a conductor, such as a wire coil, move in relationship to one another, electromagnetic induction occurs. The same holds true of a semiconductor. Group 3-5 dopants create a band gap that emits light for the auditory cortex to decipher & map frequency

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1629610736589701124>

Your cochlea is shaped this way to amplify the electromagnetic induction. Quantum biology lesson for today.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1629669761704402948>

Melanin has attracted the condensed matter
crowd. Due to its physical behavior & ability to combine w/ amorphous semiconductors that have broadband monotonic absorption from UV to NIR. It's in our ears because it can be converted from photons into phonons(Meredith et al.

# Thread: Biochemical reactions can no longer explain most of the things in biology.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1630207504989782017.html>
**Published:** 2023-02-27T03:13:44.000Z
**Tweets:** 43
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630207504989782017>

Biochemical reactions can no longer explain most of the things in biology. Everyone better make room for quantum mechanisms in their own lives. Today's PSA is on olfaction.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630208622121693185>

What if I told you that melanocytes help us smell would you believe me? Probably not, but soon I am going to tell you in a podcast that we hear using light with melanin and K+. Where did the idea come from? I read this paper in medical school. [pnas.org/doi/pdf/10.107…](https://www.pnas.org/doi/pdf/10.1073/pnas.85.1.261)

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630209261639802882>

Then in 1996 I read the work of Luca Turin and the work of Richard Axel. At the same time, we discovered a huge crater in the gulf of Mexico during surveys and a physicist thought this was tied to dinosaur extinction and mammalian dominance.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630209738720813058>

The KT event disrupted light and this lowered oxygen tensions on Earth. This would have put amazing stress on the HIF1 alpha system and the PER 2 gene class of mammals and birds. When light changes everything that relies on it changes.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630211179820793857>

Now layer this with a global sign where many people lost taste and smell due to a spike protein in the ACE 2 receptor system. A protein is known to change vibrational states in testing. This topic made me go back and think about all I learned in neurosurgery about olfaction

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630211705832611840>

Quantum biology is almost as old as quantum mechanics itself. Where quantum mechanics is often preoccupied with the interaction of light and matter, photosynthesis, the backbone of biology, is the interaction of light with living matter.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630212049618731009>

Bohr himself delivered a lecture titled ‘Light and Life’. Schrödinger, for his part, wrote What is Life? The Physical Aspect of the Living Cell, served as inspiration for the discovery of DNA.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630212548464091137>

The history of quantum biology laid out

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630213390231642112>

In a discussion of life, viruses offer a novel case study, straddling, as they do, the properties of both living and non-living systems. As such, they might also offer a novel application for quantum biology.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630213519974047744>

There has been some research investigating how quantum mechanics and viruses intersect. For example, Park et al. engineered a virus to obtain enhanced energy transport in excitonic networks.

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630213934891368448>

Quantum dots have been used to label viral proteins in an attempt to enhance live imaging of virus-host interactions. They have also been suggested to have antiviral properties. There has even been an attempt to simulate the life cycle of a virus using quantum gates.

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630214138008936448>

The vibrational theory of smell (olfaction) was first proposed by Malcolm Dyson in 1928. Many thought the Nobel Prize given in 2004 put a wrap on how humans smell. Not by a long shot.

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630214407308361729>

Charge transfer, by light, changes vibrational modes in molecules we sense. Moreover, this is also important in the context of tunneling in enzymes, which was first observed a number of decades ago.

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630214735751782402>

Chromophores in cells are a central theme with respect to quantum effects in photosynthesis and mitochondrial respiration. Many have suggested that quantum coherence might play a role in energy and charge transfer.

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630215306801971202>

This is perhaps less a unique feature of photosynthesis than it is due to the more general arrangement of chromophores in proteins. This viewpoint has shocked most conventional biologists. All these are chromophores below

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630215585442197504>

With this mindset, one must realize that all chromophores, then, would appear to be important to redox activity in biological materials. There is also a growing focus on the role that the protein scaffold (AMO Physical arrangement) plays in enhancing energy or charge transfer

### Tweet 18
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630215697174339586>

Far from the warm, wet, decoherent environment that is often cited as an argument against quantum effects in biology, the vibrational or spin states of proteins might be coupled to electronic states in a favorable way.

### Tweet 19
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630215845090586633>

Water changes its physical state when light interacts with it and this keeps the local area in a different physical state to help explain change transfer. This makes sense in the ear where the relationship of melanin to potassium chloride exists.

### Tweet 20
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630216034132058113>

KCL is a high band gap dopant that emits light with a wide band gap of 7.67 eV. Melanin can absorb every frequency of that light and the endolymph duct's water can get the rest. This mimics what water does in a nuclear reactor to control the quantum process in a reactor.

### Tweet 21
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630216213090451456>

The Interaction of light with proteins can fundamentally change the properties of a chromophore. Melanopsin, neuropsin, rhodopsin and all related opsins, for instance, absorb across a range of frequencies even though they share the same chromophore: retinal. Look below again!

### Tweet 22
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630216392216641536>

What differs in this AMO arrangement is the opsin protein, which "tunes" the chromophore’s absorption frequency.
In 2006, Researchers have demonstrated the existence of an olfactory receptor in pigment-producing cells in human skin, the so-called melanocytes.

### Tweet 23
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630216786456002560>

They have shown that the violet-like scent Beta-Ionone can activate the skin receptor. I used this in the lavender mito-hack that I wrote about on LinkedIn years ago.
[linkedin.com/pulse/lavender…](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/lavender-can-retune-our-thalamus-improve-jack-kruse/)

### Tweet 24
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630216950335799301>

Olfactory receptors are a class of ancient rhodopsin-like receptors known as G-protein coupled receptors or GPCRs. Light reception uses the same receptors in humans. So do hormones which are like ferry boats for light in our cells.

### Tweet 25
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630217040941252608>

These receptors are implicated in numerous important physiological phenomena from the regulation of inflammation to the binding of neurotransmitters in the brain. This is currently emerging as a new application of vibration-assisted electron tunneling in neuroscience.

### Tweet 26
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630217106863058944>

Electron tunneling in these contexts has been investigated as an alternative to the lock-and-key mechanism, a shape-based explanation of receptor binding.

### Tweet 27
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630217248940982272>

Sometimes bad science gets rewarded in centralized systems of belief.
Richard Axel and Linda Buck won the Nobel Prize in physiology for mapping the genes and receptors of our sense of smell.

### Tweet 28
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630217604211064832>

The 2 discovered 100s of genes code for the odorant sensors located in CN #1 of our noses. This confirms the lock and key ideas from 1928. Each receptor is a protein that changes when an odor attaches to the receptor, causing an electrical signal to be sent to the brain.

### Tweet 29
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630217749753372674>

That is the belief today in centralized Ivory towers of academia. Differences between odorant sensors mean that certain odors cause a signal to be released from a certain receptor. We are then able to interpret varying signals from our receptors as specific scents.

### Tweet 30
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630218082944786433>

Was the “shape theory” not validated as a foregone conclusion or just the most palatable of theories?

### Tweet 31
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630218200792158208>

What you might not know is many of Axel's friends in science went out to prove Turin's ideas wrong. So far to date not one of them has proven Turin wrong. In fact, many results in biophysics are making him look right and making Axel and Buck look like frauds.

### Tweet 32
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630218344719589376>

Human smell perception is not ONLY influenced by the shape of the odor molecule but by oscillations in which electrons will quantum tunnel across energy gaps in the olfactory receptors.

### Tweet 33
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630218428328951809>

This decentralized idea has a lot of brilliant ideas in it because we know DNA, photosynthesis, bone, and enzymes all use the same effects. These effects are also found in the mitochondrial of olfactory nerves in the human brain.

### Tweet 34
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630218517189459971>

The exact mechanism by which we recognize different scent molecules is one of the great mysteries of our physiology. Many people in centralized science think this science is settled since Richard Axel was given and Noble Prize in 2004 for the lock and key model of olfaction.

### Tweet 35
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630218735481942016>

Many Nobel Prizes have been given for a half-truth. While a complete explanation of our sense of smell may fall under the purview of biology, the fundamental physical phenomena that we are in fact detecting in labs today....

### Tweet 36
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630218867061538817>

after this Prize was given years ago might be only explainable by quantum mechanics. I used to believe in Axel's work. I no longer do. I believe human smell perception is governed by quantum spin-residual information.

### Tweet 37
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630219037610332160>

Recently researchers (2020) used infrared light to excite the molecular bond oscillation in odor molecules. As the molecules absorb the light, they begin to oscillate and emit an infrared photon, which contains spin-residual information.

### Tweet 38
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630219128488206336>

To test the differences in olfactory perception of molecules at different levels of excitation, 23 human subjects were given two different scents to smell, one citrusy and the other musky.

### Tweet 39
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630219216887398401>

Each smell was illuminated under three different conditions: two different infrared wavelengths and a non-infrared light outside of the visible spectrum for control.

### Tweet 40
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630219298349146126>

The subjects were asked to smell each scenario 10 times and rate the intensity of their smell perception for each inhale with an integer value. The first five whiffs of each test had no illumination, and the second five had one of the three illumination conditions.

### Tweet 41
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630219384026279939>

Each subject’s fifth inhale intensity was normalized to an intensity value of 10, and the non-infrared data was subtracted from the excited data to correct for psychological effects.

### Tweet 42
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630219501663838209>

The researchers found in all scenarios that the intensity of the smell was affected by external infrared illumination. To them, this means molecular bonding vibrations and spin residuals play a role in smell perception.
BOOM

### Tweet 43
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630219634568835072>

The pandemic only reinforced that belief. The spike protein changes the vibration of the scent receptors in the olfactory nerve to cause the loss of taste and smell. Who do you believe now?
[academic.oup.com/chemse/article…](https://academic.oup.com/chemse/article/21/6/773/488342)

### Tweet 44
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1630219968838000641>

That ends today's lesson on quantum biology. If you like it come follow my work on Patreon at [patreon.com/DrJackKruse](http://www.patreon.com/DrJackKruse)
