# Thread: A.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1627826337808359424.html>
**Published:** 2023-02-20T17:29:11.000Z
**Tweets:** 23
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627826337808359424>

A. Why is a diamond colorless? Diamond is a crystal of carbon. Does the color of diamonds have something to do with the physics of the lattice carbon is in?

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627828685058322434>

B. Are diamonds a semiconductor? Yes. In addition to being the ultimate heat-flux substrate, diamond as a semiconductor outperforms silicon by a factor of 23,000 times, GaN by a factor of 120, and SiC by a factor of 40 thanks to its unique material properties.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627829274068525058>

C. As a result, diamonds can rule high-power-density applications. Diamonds are a large band gap semiconductors. [diamondfoundry.com/pages/diamond-…](https://diamondfoundry.com/pages/diamond-semiconductor-technology)

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627829894850793472>

D. The size of the band gap of a semiconductor determines its color. Did you know that? Because of the large band gap, such as the 5.4 eV of diamond, then no light in the visible spectrum can be absorbed and it appears transparent.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627831384189329411>

E. These substances transmit all incident light and are colorless in their pure forms. In their powdered forms, or when their structure prevents light from being transmitted, all light is reflected back to the observer and we see them as white.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627832629847367682>

F. "Large band gap semiconductors" are excellent insulators and behave like covalently bonded materials. What are some covalently bonded materials in cells that are semiconductors?

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627833564866789377>

G. The most common protein in humans is collagen and it is a large bandwidth semiconductor. Current-sensing atomic force spectroscopy (CSAFS) shows the electronic band gap of collagen is present in a variety of experimental conditions. [pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31404493/](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31404493/)

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627834447046270976>

H. Collagen color is off-white because the dopant it uses in cells is copper. The small addition of copper changes its color in tissues. A tissue palette is rich with colors, some of which arise because of the size of band gaps in natural semiconductors. Liver color = iron

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627835743056936961>

I. The mechanism behind the color we perceive in semiconductors can be explained by the band theory that governs color in many metals. According to band theory, the electrons in some solids behave differently than those in individual atoms.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627836038717624320>

J. In a single atom, an electron can occupy one of several distinct energy levels. When light strikes the surface, electrons in a lower energy level can be excited to a higher energy level. The distance between levels represents the relative energy required to excite an electron

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627836160956411907>

K. As atoms are brought closer together, these distinct energy levels interact. The low energy levels overlap and the high energy levels overlap. As the number of neighboring atoms increases, more overlap occurs and bands of low and high energy replace the distinct energy levels

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627837079592878082>

L. Do you know the thyroid gland uses iodine as a semiconductive atom because it brings atoms closer together to proton tunnel? The numbers in T3 and T4 represent the iodine atoms in those hormones. [sciencedirect.com/science/articl…](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S001085452030062X)

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627838110779293696>

M. The remarkably efficient charge transport of iodine is unexpected but observed when iodine is added to iodide-based ionic liquids. It appears to be based on a bond-exchange process that resembles the Grotthuss mechanism for protons diffusion in water. [en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grotthuss…](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grotthuss_mechanism)

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627839305308680193>

N. Did you know a painter’s palette is rich with colors, some of which arise from the band gaps in natural semiconductors. In cadmium yellow, the 2.6 eV band gap energy permits absorption of violet some blue, but none of the other colors. Only light above 2.6 eV is absorbed.

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627840108496814080>

O. We see it as a yellow color, as shown by the color scale below. DaVinci knew that adding atoms changed cadmium yellow. He did not know that the addition of more metal reduced the band gap of the paint and this changed to light absorbed.

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627840364533936129>

P. An illustration of this change in the band gap size is shown above by using mixed crystals of yellow cadmium sulfide (CdS, Eg = 2.6 eV), and black cadmium selenide (CdSe, Eg = 1 .6 eV), which have the same structure and form a solid-solution series.

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627840749059407873>

Q. The photograph also illustrates the yellow-orange-red-black sequence of these mixed crystals as the band-gap energy decreases.
Mixed crystals such as cadmium sulfoselenide (Cd4SSe3) form the painter’s pigment cadmium orange and are also used to color glass & plastic artwork

### Tweet 18
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627841089632669701>

R. Mercuric sulfide (HgS) exists in two different crystalline forms. Cinnabar has Eg = 2.0 eV is a deep red, but can transform upon exposure to light in an improperly formulated paint to the black metacinnabar with Eg = 1.6 eV in as little as five years.

### Tweet 19
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627842501833793536>

S. It happened in DaVinci's masterpiece below & it has happened in a number of other old paintings. [en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint\_Joh…](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saint_John_the_Baptist_\(Leonardo\))

### Tweet 20
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627842994949816321>

T. Cells and SV engineers use another method of manipulating the color of semiconductor materials by adding impurities. These doped semiconductors have energy levels within the band gap and allow cells/us to tailor the wavelength of emitted light.

### Tweet 21
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627846450582282240>

U. Some natural semiconductors contain impurities in their natural state, providing useful insights into these behaviors. Cells seem to have figured this out long ago because copper ions are used as dopants in bond collagen. [semanticscholar.org/paper/The-dist…](https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/The-distribution-of-trace-metal-ions-in-bone-and-Spadaro-Becker/0f477af5bfe2f7c70caf48fa301d00a075f8c621)

### Tweet 22
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627847317112995841>

V. In this seminal book Robert O. Becker found that copper was the dopant used in bone collagen which was acting as the N-semiconductor. Apatite was the P-type semiconductor. See pgs 126-135 Cu pegs on pg 135. [issuu.com/urbanlaughters…](https://issuu.com/urbanlaughtersuite/docs/57661030-the-body-electric)

### Tweet 23
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1627847739299057664>

X. I hope you enjoyed this lesson on solid-state physics showing up in biology proving that Nature built semiconductive circuits long before man did in Silicon Valley.

New death data in UK bad for the experimental group. The New UK data confirms recent trends all over Western Europe, including the Netherlands and Switzerland. They are dying.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1618013849864798208>

The Office of National Statistics said it had registered 17,381 deaths in England and Wales in the week ended Friday, January 13. That figure is about 20 percent more than the five-year average - and 30 percent more than longer-term averages - for the year’s second week.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1618014000683573248>

Only about 650 of the deaths had Covid as an underlying cause, the government said, so most of the excess was not related to Covid.

With recent upward revisions, Europe will now probably report more excess deaths in 2022 than either 2020, before Covid vaccines were available, or 2021, before boosters began in earnest.

No direct connection has yet been proven but the signal is strong for \\/ etiology
But in contrast, South Africa and Bulgaria - two middle-income countries with much lower levels of Covid vaccinations - have reported normal or below-normal deaths for several months.

# Thread: Organisms are open systems dependent on energy flow.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1614346045345107968.html>
**Published:** 2023-01-11T22:13:52.000Z
**Tweets:** 50
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614346045345107968>

Organisms are open systems dependent on energy flow. Energy flows in together with materials, & waste products are exported, along with the spent energy that goes to make up entropy. And that is how living systems can, in principle, escape from the second law of thermodynamics

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614351737418092545>

The second law, as you will recall, encapsulates the monotonous, unexceptional experience of everyday life: that all physical systems run down, ultimately decaying to homogeneous disorganization when all useful energy is finally exhausted, being converted into entropy.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614352205552680962>

Organisms appear anti-entropic while alive. Not only do they keep their organization intact, but also manage to have lots of energy for their activities. But how do they really manage their anti-entropic existence? What would a thermodynamic description of organisms be like?

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614352761952546817>

Many physicists/chemists have stressed the importance of energy flow and dissipation to the organization of living things. But, in order to benefit from energy flow, the system must have some means of capturing and storing the energy.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614353088407633920>

Atomic organization lifts life up from thermodynamic equilibrium. Solar energy flows through Mars and Venus just as it flows through Earth, yet only Earth can capture the energy by means of the chlorophyll of green plants.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614353423343964160>

In common with all other organisms, green plants have an impressive metabolic network of biochemical reactions “downstream’’ of the energy capture, which stores the energy, not just for the plants, but also to pass along to the other organisms in the food web.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614353670598189056>

The entire ecosystem on earth is one big energy store maintained far away from thermodynamic equilibrium.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614354084760551424>

The key to understanding the thermodynamics of living organisms is, therefore, neither energy flow nor energy dissipation science, but energy storage under energy flow. That is what AMO physics is all about. Nature specializes in it.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614354539464855552>

Energy flow is of NO consequence unless the energy is trapped within the system where it circulates, to build up structures for storing the energy, and to do work before it is dissipated.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614354693794193413>

An organism arises when the loop of circulating energy somehow closes on itself to give a regenerating, reproducing life- cycle within which energy is mobilized, remaining stored as it is mobilized.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614355388182286338>

The energy goes into complex cascades of coupled cyclic processes within the system before it's allowed to dissipate to the outside. These cascades of cycles span the entire gamut of space-times from slow to fast, from local to global, that all together, make up the life-cycle

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614355790235582464>

Each cycle is simply a domain of coherent energy storage. As you will recall, coherent energy is energy that can do work because it is all coming and going together. Water & sunlight create coherent domains in cells.

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614355968929800192>

The cycles within the life cycle are all interlinked in a very special way, in order to feed off the one-way energy flow. Picture them as a complicated system of eddies forming in a pool off the main river stream.

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614356909066448898>

The many-fold cycles of life coupled to energy flow in life = ΔG positive which are designed to couple to the thermodynamically downhill ones, i.e., those yielding energy =ΔG negative. Humans can break this coupling with bad designs around light

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614357513406750721>

The ATP/ADP couple, ubiquitous to the living system, effectively turns all biosynthetic and other energy-requiring uphill reactions downhill. Life is literally downhill, or, on a more optimistic and cheery note, effortless and spontaneous, all the way

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614358769261068292>

That living processes are organized in cycles is also intuitively obvious by a casual examination of the metabolic chart in any biochemistry book. Biochemistry alone is impotent to explain life without the photons levers it requires to explain its enzymatic flux/storage.

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614359049835008000>

Apart from the prominent cycles such as the tricarboxylic acid cycle and the cyclic interconversion of ATP/ADP, NADH/NAD and other redox (reduction-oxidation) intermediates, many more cycles and epicycles are entangled in the metabolic network.

### Tweet 18
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614359516857999363>

Another prominent way in which cycles appear in living systems is the wide spectrum of biological rhythms — with periods ranging from milliseconds for electrical discharges of single cells to circadian and circa-annual cycles in whole organisms and populations of organisms.

### Tweet 19
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614359773754925057>

These cycles interlock with metabolic cycles to give the organism a complex, multidimensional, entangled space-time, very far removed from the simple, linear Newtonian space and time of mechanical physics.

### Tweet 20
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614360220712550406>

There are some very suggestive observations that all these solar/dark rhythms are indeed entangled, or coupled together.

### Tweet 21
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614360730895007748>

Geneticists have discovered that mutations in two genes of Drosophila, period and timeless, which speed up, slow down, or abolish circadian rhythm, also cause corresponding changes in the millisecond wing beat cycle of the male fly's love song!

### Tweet 22
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614360915737985024>

This correlation spans seven orders of magnitude of characteristic timescales, reflecting the full extent of coupled energy storage and mobilization in the living system.

### Tweet 23
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614361508426792961>

Energy is stored and mobilized over all space-times according to the relaxation times and volumes of the processes involved. The result is that organisms can take advantage of two different ways to mobilize energy with maximum efficiency

### Tweet 24
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614361953131978754>

First, non-equilibrium transfer in which stored energy (light) is transferred before it is thermalized, & quasi-equilibrium transfer, which is slow enough to allow all thermalized, and other exchanging energies to equilibrate, for which the free energy change approaches zero

### Tweet 25
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614362395001888769>

This occurs in accordance with conventional thermodynamic considerations. Because all the modes of activity are coupled, the energy input into any mode is readily shared or delocalized over all modes, and conversely, energy from all modes can become concentrated into any mode

### Tweet 26
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614362605950205953>

Another way to express the same thing is that energy from any point can spread throughout the system, or become concentrated at any point from all over the system. In technical terms, energy coupling in the living system is symmetrical.

### Tweet 27
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614363197116383233>

Symmetrical energy coupling & cyclical flows are both predicted from the thermodynamics of the steady state, in the form, of Onsager's reciprocity relationship & of Morowitz's theorem. Do your centralized MD talk about this with you? If not why did you hire them?

### Tweet 28
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614364097893773317>

Decentralized clinicians like me teach our patients that Onsager's reciprocity relationship states that for a system of many coupled linear flows under conjugate forces will always be symmetric mathematically.

### Tweet 29
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614364653613682690>

Morowitz's theorem states that the flow of energy through the system from a source (sun) to a sink (cell) will lead to at least one cycle in the system at a steady state, provided that the energy is trapped and stored within the system.

### Tweet 30
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614365547172614145>

This important theorem is the only attempt to account for cycles in the living system, it implies that the steady state — at which global balance is maintained — must harbor nonlinear processes. Non-linear processes in cells is all carried about by UV bio-photons.

### Tweet 31
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614365812118134784>

Nearly 100 years ago the Russian biologist A. Gurwitsch put forth the hypothesis that weak ultraviolet radiation comes out from the living tissues, and it is responsible for the regulation of cell growth. He called this radiation “mitogenetic radiation”.

### Tweet 32
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614366447769206787>

However, this interesting result was forgotten by the scientific community and mitogenetic radiation has been considered an artifact for many years. Many papers like this one below are in the literature.

### Tweet 33
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614366806084575232>

With the improvement of the methods to detect the weak level of light radiation emitted from living cells & the increase in understanding of quantum optics, there has been a renewed interest in this phenomenon.

### Tweet 34
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614367199119958016>

Colli & Facchini in the 1950s and Fritz Popp in the 1980s did extensive work in this field to understand in detail the origin and the meaning of such ultra-weak emissions, called bio-photons. Cells/blood are plasma that mimics the sun's ability optically.

### Tweet 35
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614369015782162433>

Bio-photons are an endogenous production of photons in and from cells and organisms, and this emission is characteristic of alive organisms. This emission is entirely different from the normal bioluminescence observed in some simple as well as complex organisms in the sea.

### Tweet 36
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614372120456499201>

Biophotons are a 1000 X weaker. It's present in all living organisms from plants to human beings! [sciencealert.com/you-can-t-see-…](https://www.sciencealert.com/you-can-t-see-it-but-humans-actually-glow-in-visible-light)

### Tweet 37
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614372570622574594>

It's also different from thermal radiation because its intensity is many orders of magnitude ^ than the one calculated by Planck’s law in the visible energy range @ room temps.

### Tweet 38
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614372719478407169>

The main characteristics of biophotons measured up to now are the following: the total intensity of the emission is quite low and goes from several to some hundred photons/sec per cm2 surface of the living system.

### Tweet 39
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614373089327960066>

The spectral intensity is quite flat for wavelengths ranging between 200 and 800 nm. After any type of stress (chemical agents, excitation by any light, temperature) the emission increases by almost a factor of 10 and relaxes to the normal values quite slowly.

### Tweet 40
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614373258886873092>

It follows hyperbolic functions rather than an exponential law. Finally, the photocount statistics that account for the probability of having N photons within some time interval seem to follow a Poisson distribution.

### Tweet 41
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614373655550599168>

Poisson distribution tells us all life is based on the probabilities in light frequencies. The biophoton emission is also very sensitive to the biological characteristics of the system under examination.

### Tweet 42
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614373993670213634>

Moreover, for this reason, these types of measures have been successfully applied to medical diagnostics, agriculture, and ecology today but not to centralized healthcare because it would limit Pharma profits

### Tweet 43
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614374986545782787>

Onsager's reciprocity relationship is coupled to biophoton release & his theory has been extended to the far from equilibrium regime for systems containing many enzymes working together (TCA cycle) and more recently, for “infinite” (or sufficiently large) quantum systems!

### Tweet 44
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614375501241487360>

I believe some form of Onsager's reciprocity relationship holds in all living systems to account for the ready mobilization of energy — why we can have energy at will and.....

### Tweet 45
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614375636520554496>

— for the linear relationships between steady-state flows and thermodynamic forces outside the range of equilibrium which we have actually observed in many biological systems.

### Tweet 46
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614377535982239744>

Linearity in biological processes can arise in enzymes operating near a “multidimensional inflection point” (i.e., a local minimum or maximum) far away from thermodynamic equilibrium, if some of the rate constants of different reactions are linked. Light links all enzymes.

### Tweet 47
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614378433714200577>

Implications? If your metabolic cycles remain coupled properly to the circadian cycles of the sun you will remain healthy because of the ideas buried in the science of Onsager's reciprocity.

### Tweet 48
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614378686425321472>

In the properly coupled cell, the living system is a superposition of non-dissipative cyclic processes and dissipative irreversible processes, so that Onsager's reciprocity relationship applies only to the former.

### Tweet 49
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614379188462436353>

In other words, it applies to coupled processes for which the net entropy production is balanced out to zero. Cells that are coupled to light and dark cycles have no problem gaining a negative entropy state while living. I hope you like this walk thru quantum biology.

### Tweet 50
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1614379506298494977>

If you did join me on Patreon = [patreon.com/DrJackKruse](http://patreon.com/DrJackKruse) or at [kruseatdestin.com](http://kruseatdestin.com) to learn more. Thanks. Share this so we can decentralize healthcare!

# Thread: Energy transfer via heat, on which the science of thermodynamics is based, is by far the least efficient and nonspecific
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1612609390862864384.html>
**Published:** 2023-01-10T14:38:50.000Z
**Tweets:** 5
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1612609390862864384>

Energy transfer via heat, on which the science of thermodynamics is based, is by far the least efficient and nonspecific form of transfer. The biosphere does not make its living by absorbing heat from the environment.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1612609965574742016>

No organism can live like a heat engine, nor can it obtain its energy or negative entropy by feeding on carbon or diamond pulp and burning it with oxygen.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1612610120462270464>

Instead, life depends on catching an excited electron quite precisely — by means of specific light absorbing pigments — and then tapping off its energy as it falls back towards the ground state.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1612610667508555776>

Life uses the highest grade of energy, the packet or quantum size of which is sufficient to cause specific motion of electrons in the outer orbitals of molecules.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1612611269894225921>

It is on account of this that living systems can populate their high energy levels without heating up the body excessively, and hence contribute to what Schrödinger intuitively identifies as ‘negative entropy’.

# Thread: Today's PSA on wisdom.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1612821272362188801.html>
**Published:** 2023-01-10T00:36:54.000Z
**Tweets:** 11
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1612821272362188801>

Today's PSA on wisdom. Here is the question.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1612821375630049280>

My answer: The wisdom we lack, in all probability, will always exceed the wisdom that we have! This creates the pressures we sense in life to motivate us to action by thinking.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1612821489849438269>

It's far too easy to confuse the difference between knowledge and wisdom. The dark TV saga, Breaking Bad, is mostly about that difference: it's one thing to know how to do something, but it's another thing to know when/why to do it.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1612821600591552512>

Do you you believe wisdom is only born from knowledge, or is wisdom the insights gained from that knowledge? The physics of organisms tells me life transmits key knowledge to its offspring by design.
Why?

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1612821708930707458>

If you were reading my work on Patreon this answer to this question would be intuitive. Modern physics now has proven that energy and information are equivalent in physics. Landau & Shannon's work wass critical in making this linkage. Can I explain this to you simply?

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1612821800651464705>

‘Negative entropy’ does not have much to do with free energy as Schrödinger indicated in his 1944 book on 'What Is Life', instead living things create a negative entropy illusion with the way energy is trapped, stored and mobilized in the living system (cell).

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1612821888396402689>

If energy is trapped by cells, then we know by defintition when life is born it already has wisdom in it. Modern quantum biology has experimentally proven that energy is trapped directly at the electronic level in cells.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1612821964413931524>

Energy is stored not only as vibrational and electronic bond energies in biochemicals, but also in the structure of the system: its membranes, and in gradients, fields and flow patterns, compartments, organelles, cell water and tissues.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1612822049017270273>

All this in turn enables organisms to mobilize their energies coherently at any time it is needed and hence make available the entire spectrum of stored energies for physiological work.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1612822327506386945>

It is energy on demand by atomic design = wisdom on demand is possible because of this arrangement even before the offspring has launched its first thought in life.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1612823772922347520>

If you like this thread thank [@ShellyLegit](https://twitter.com/ShellyLegit) because she wanted to know why she instinctively put her head out the window in cold weather during a migraine fit. This is my response to her. She came into this world with this wisdom.

**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1609588526227529729>

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1609588826325880840>

Balance is a misnomer in life. We can achieve solid integration. Your hand opens & closes. If it were always a fist or always stretched open, you would be paralyzed. Your design is found in contracting and expanding like two beautifully balanced and coordinated bird's wings

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1609589182908829697>

Many people seek balance when they should seek anti-fragility in centralized healthcare systems. When we integrate life, work, rest, and play it allows us to accept the dynamics life deals.

To help with our integration consider who matters most and what matters most, your values, passion, and purpose. Base your decisions using those as markers. Do this consistently and you begin to enjoy a fully integrated life.

Find what works for you and your key stakeholders and live, lead and work to attain and maintain that as best you can.

2 Because if you are making mistakes, then you're making new things, trying new things, learning, living, pushing yourself, changing yourself, & changing your world. You're doing things you've never done before, and, you're doing something new, rejecting your old ways & adapting

# Thread: H2O is the third most common molecule in the Universe (following the H2 & CO molecule), and its standard chemical struct
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1604681141767864320.html>
**Published:** 2022-12-21T03:34:58.000Z
**Tweets:** 5
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1604681141767864320>

H2O is the third most common molecule in the Universe (following the H2 & CO molecule), and its standard chemical structure, based on the hydrogen bond, is actually confined by a simple scheme of charges interacting via static Coulomb forces

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1604683080442253313>

That is, H2O is totally reliant on electrostatics and omits all mention of electrodynamics and the consequent radiation field.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1604683446265303040>

It has been speculated that a good percentage of effects in condensed matter physics make use of the radiation field in one way or another but it still doesn’t seem to have found a place in much of basic chemistry.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1604683807298187265>

In biological systems almost all water is within a fraction of a micron or less from a surface or molecular backbone of collagen, which behaves in a quantum way, where the Coulomb law of electrostatics does not apply.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1604684783119917058>

In these circumstances, like charges begin to attract. Biology itself depends on this ability because it allows the accumulation of tissues from negatively charged cell bodies

# Thread: Decentralized MDs vs centralized MD example: DeMed MDs know that the optic nerve can be used as the entry point to treat
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1605406447533436928.html>
**Published:** 2022-12-19T03:32:52.000Z
**Tweets:** 35
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1605406447533436928>

Decentralized MDs vs centralized MD example: DeMed MDs know that the optic nerve can be used as the entry point to treat Parkinson's disease using light. Centralized MDs have no idea this is possible.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1605406930406871040>

One way in which solar exposure is beneficial in Parkinson’s is light exposure through to the eyes! Bright-solar light therapy has been shown to benefit PD patients, improving sleep, mood, and also motor function.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1605407542179692545>

A remarkable study was able to measure the amount of light reaching the mesencephalon (the midbrain, which houses the substantia nigra dopamine neurons) when the light was shone on the eyes.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1605408149334331394>

They observed a sharp peak at around 710 nanometers, which is in the range of infrared light. Full-spectrum sunlight stimulates the synthesis of neuromelanin in the midbrain, just as it stimulates the synthesis of melanin in the skin (tan).

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1605408880959410180>

In the midbrain at the substantia nigra a return of the brownish tan color of the brain tells us that dopamine neurons are regenerating. Just as the sun tans your skin it can tan your midbrain = low Vit D levels is a marker for PD

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1605409378407895040>

The neuromelanin created in the brain protects the dopaminergic neurons from oxidative damage by mopping up free radicals. Melatonin made in the local mitochondria helps this process.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1605410101300297728>

When using artificial light to treat PD, the light should be pulsed via the optic nerve because artificial light is frequency deficient, has flicker, and is polarized. Pulsing the light offsets some of these risks to deliver more light to the substantial nigra.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1605411085384826880>

Sunlight creates electric fields in our atmosphere every day and sunlight creates a Coulomb force in the retina and all the visual tracts that connect to the midbrain. The Coulomb force is an electrostatic force.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1605411481591611392>

Tinnitus is another disease that can be helped by light treatment because the cochlea has melanin in it and if that pigment is missing the reflex loop is damaged and the sensation of noise happens when the Coulomb force in the cochlea is lost.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1605411767068545024>

Electrostatic phenomena arise from the forces that electric charges exert on each other. Such forces are described by Coulomb's law. Sunlight increases the Coulomb force in tissues.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1605411931178803201>

Even though electrostatically induced forces seem to be rather weak because of how we experience them in life, when the scale of their action shrinks, as it does in a cell, the electrostatic force increases tremendously in its power.

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1605412227158269952>

This is great news for disease reversal. Decentralized MDs know this. Centralized MDs have no idea about how light influences the charge density of tissue with mitochondrial damage.

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1605412429302616065>

For example, some electrostatic forces such as the one between an electron & a proton, that together make up a hydrogen atom in mitochondria, are about 36 orders of magnitude stronger than the gravitational force acting between them.

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1605413000621563904>

This is why sunlight is the biggest asymmetric treatment and can offer a patient with lowered Coulomb charge. The electrostatic force generated in your skin is another example of a strong electrostatic force because the skin, as an insulator can hold large amounts of charge

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1605413524792086528>

Coulomb forces must be balanced after daytime and this occurs at night when light is absent. This is why light at night is so bad for us, and why a centralized MD is impotent to figure out why these maladies occur.

# Thread: Most have no Earthly idea of the non-linear effects of light-affected atoms on the building found on Earth.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1598798355307515904.html>
**Published:** 2022-11-30T12:30:07.000Z
**Tweets:** 47
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598798355307515904>

Most have no Earthly idea of the non-linear effects of light-affected atoms on the building found on Earth. Consider the pyrimidine base normally found in all life forms' DNA below.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598798744064913408>

If one looks at a pyrimidine base in DNA one would see the pyridine ring is made of 5 equally charged carbon atoms and a single nitrogen atom that has a relatively positive charge compared to the neutral carbons as the picture shows.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598799190666141696>

The charge density in the molecule is not homogenous. When light strikes it, it alters that charge density further. Implications for circadian timing are even bigger. Why?

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598799460636786688>

The varying electric charge tells us that each atom in the base ring of this molecule experiences time differently when light radiation interacts with its atoms, but few realize why time is relative in this molecule.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598799762047713282>

This relationship is present for every single organic chemical used inside a cell. The positive charge of nitrogen causes the ring to develop a dynamic positive valency of all the solo atoms in the pyridine ring.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598800130546835468>

People forget nitrogen is the most common element in our atmosphere at 71%. It turns out this change in the charge of nitrogen is important in the formation of clouds from extraterrestrial cosmic radiation too.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598800641685540864>

Nitrogen in the atmosphere is triple bonded. Cosmic radiation is capable of breaking that bond & in doing so, the electricity created in collision creates the electrostatic binding that makes the "water seeds" allow rain to be made to allow photosynthesis on Earth to occur

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598801327022231553>

Remember that the ring structure of life also uses nitrogen too in its core. Might this be how sunlight is first changed into life's first electric signal allowing atoms to become biotic?

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598801625077862400>

You need to look at the whole of the ring of pyrimidine over the atoms to see what nature is really up to, as she creates the arrow of time in the living state. It's not what most think. Photons from our star or from space come to our pyridine rings in a TIMELESS state. Why?

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598801883405197325>

Photons do not experience time. They are packets of energy that have no mass and must constantly move. Photons interact with electrons in matter. All parts of the electromagnetic spectrum interact with matter differently = relativity

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598802439133548544>

This "light relativity" allows for different charge changes in the organic matter that a cell can use.

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598802864486293504>

Once the photon interacts with the electrons in the ring, time begins to manifest, and it manifests in a way tied to how this charge is distributed in the DNA base ring. This is why tryptophan is a time crystal in some of the most important organic molecules in mitochondria

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598802977296457737>

The classic formula and 2-D drawing of the pyrimidine chemical ring above gives you an idea of shape and chemistry, but it never reveals why or how nature is really using it. This is why medicine is fundamentally blind to the actions of light in most things in biology.

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598803286332776454>

The 2-D formula tells & predicts the dipole moment of the ring, but the atomic diagram gives every chemical a personality and profile that has a high degree of specificity and sensitivity = which we call its quantization.

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598803623625965568>

A molecule's ability to be quantized & coherently controlled by our sun makes life possible. Atoms grouped together in this way begin to behave collectively/coherently in ways that examining the parts would never reveal; this is why nature is a mystery to modern science

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598803878119473152>

They have no idea how the changing charge of atoms alters atomic structure to make abiotic atoms behave collectively to become biotic and assume a light/circadian time stamp as the light that programs matter slows. It is only when light slows that the arrow of time appears

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598804629696946176>

It turns out that how the electric charge density is made & varies is the key to understanding what nature is doing inside of cells.

Thunderstorms, WiFi, 5G are capable of altering the charge density in cells therefore they all have biologic effects.

### Tweet 18
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598805133185490956>

One does not need ionization to change life's trajectory as most believe. The entire spectrum has different ways to change the gear shifts in cells. It is time we wake up to it. This is why the NTP study showed 2G RF radiations could cause cancer in nocturnal animals.

### Tweet 19
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598805396168134656>

The circadian clock regulates many transcriptional–translational processes influencing whole-cell metabolism and particularly mitochondrial activity. It uses light variations to get the job done.

### Tweet 20
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1598805691850186754>

I hope you enjoyed this thread. If you'd like to learn more about my work join my Patreon classroom. Thanks! [patreon.com/DrJackKruse](http://www.patreon.com/DrJackKruse)

### Tweet 21
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599033121738960896>

Now, I'd like to build on this lesson. Can the first thread of 20 Tweets help you understand how a lack of UV and IR-A light trapped in cell water could lead to a Warburg metabolism that changes lactate conversion into pyruvate in a Warburg metabolism? I think so.

### Tweet 22
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599033479379189761>

NADPH is critical in recycling to maintain the negative charge density inside a cell. It is the key to a circadian-controlled redox state. What happens when sunlight is absent?

### Tweet 23
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599034029730238465>

The data imply that at citrate synthase, the majority of acetyl-CoA is derived from glucose, whereas the majority of OAA is derived from glutamine. Do you know what stressed cells do metabolically? They release a ton of lactate.

### Tweet 24
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599035041572884481>

Because glucose & glutamine are rapidly consumed during the proliferation of most cell types, the results may reflect a general phenomenon during rapid cell growth. Lactate can become pyruvate by the action of lactate dehydrogenase. Pyruvate then enters the mitochondria

### Tweet 25
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599035842252574720>

The charge density inside the mitochondria during this step is key. Pyruvate is usually acted on by pyruvate dehydrogenase and thiamine to enter the TCA cycle. Without UV and IR-A light this does not happen the way your biochemistry books say it does.

### Tweet 26
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599036121383505921>

For normal energy synthesis, pyruvate enters the mitochondria by active transport with the help of pyruvate translocase, a transport protein. Pyruvate is utilized in the citric acid cycle to produce acetyl CoA. Pyr translocases charge density is altered by light

### Tweet 27
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599037216046866432>

What can a variable charge density do? When light is missing in action PDH isn't the decision maker of the fate of pyruvate in cells. Pyruvate decarboxylase is.

### Tweet 28
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599037614438330368>

Pyruvate dehydrogenase (PDH) is a convergence point in the regulation of the metabolic fine tuning between glucose and FA oxidation. PDH converts pyruvate to acetyl-CoA, and thereby increases the influx of acetyl-CoA from glycolysis into the TCA cycle = normal charge density

### Tweet 29
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599037978331865089>

Pyruvate carboxylase (PC) is a biotin-containing enzyme that catalyzes the HCO3−& MgATP-dependent carboxylation of pyruvate to form oxaloacetate. This is an anaplerotic reaction, replenishing oxaloacetate withdrawn from the TCA cycle for various pivotal biochemical pathways

### Tweet 30
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599039342567391232>

Implications of the light decision? Research has detected the entry of pyruvate into the TCA cycle via PDH but not PC is normal in high redox states. Exit of intermediates from the TCA cycle supplied fatty acid synthesis, and some 60% of fatty acyl carbon is glucose-derived.

### Tweet 31
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599040106090070022>

Cells must also allocate pyruvate molecules into appropriate metabolic pathways to produce and maintain pools of intermediates needed to synthesize macromolecules. Pyruvate is in a superposition state. What controls its metabolic fate?

### Tweet 32
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599040308666576896>

ANSWER: The charge density of the translocase, and the presence or absence of thiamine and biotin in a cell.

### Tweet 33
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599040530507177984>

Therefore, a complete understanding of the biology of cell proliferation will require a comprehensive understanding of the regulation of metabolic fluxes. Charge density effects of light on the translocase is absent today in biology

### Tweet 34
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599041748285935616>

When the charge density is altered by a low redox state of translocase the entry of pyruvate into the tricarboxylic acid (TCA) cycle via pyruvate dehydrogenase (PDH) rises to 100%. No pyruvate carboxylase activity occurs. This changes the reality of the cell.

### Tweet 35
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599042674576588800>

In this low redox state, with PDH flux at 100% the exit of intermediates from the TCA cycle is changed. Only 60% of fatty acyl carbon is glucose-derived in this case. Where does the rest of the substrate come from when sunlight is deficient in the system?

### Tweet 36
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599043227843108866>

Glutamine is a non-essential amino acid that can become conditionally essential under catabolic stress conditions = low redox state of pyruvate translocase. The picture is another reminder of the effect of light stress.

### Tweet 37
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599043927566270465>

Glutamine can play a major role in replenishing TCA cycle intermediates for ATP production and serves as an important carbon and nitrogen donor for the synthesis of proteins, lipids, and nucleotides. This is NOT something high redox cells with large NADPH stores rely on.

### Tweet 38
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599044261755834368>

Glutamine use is a salvage pathway from older evolutionary metabolic pathway days when the sun had a different spectrum before the Cambrian explosion.

### Tweet 39
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599045320582627328>

Post Cambrian explosion sunlight had 10% more UV light in it and this fueled more complex life and faster metabolism. Glucose & glutamine are two of the most abundant nutrients in plasma, and together they account for most carbon and nitrogen metabolism in mammalian cells.

### Tweet 40
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599045635847557120>

Post Cambrian explosion life on Earth = more rapid cell proliferation became the norm. This has been associated with a robust but apparently wasteful metabolism of glucose photosynthetically in mammalian cells that had to be controlled.

### Tweet 41
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599046080725159937>

In the mammalian eye we saw many versions of evolutionary change to the retina as sunlight change. Even today, the human retina uses this metabolic shift to fuel rapid cell growth, but it does cause abnormal cancerous degeneration when sunlight is present.

### Tweet 42
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599046470832750592>

The charge density of sunlight constrains oncogenic potential in the retina. Yet, in many other cell lines that have had oncogenic change, we now see this WARBURG metabolism manifest abnormally with uncontrolled cell growth.

### Tweet 43
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599046985448685569>

Without proper light, glutamine metabolism can serve to maintain pools of nonessential amino acids. Glutamine was also used to replenish TCA cycle intermediates = anaplerosis.

### Tweet 44
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599047464300212224>

Surprisingly to researchers and your biochemistry textbook dogma, glutamine catabolism can provide cells with a robust source of NADPH production to maintain redox power inside of the mitochondria while outside charge density collapses (cytosol)

### Tweet 45
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599048202904223749>

Implications are the majority of glutamine-derived carbon will be secreted as lactate in Warburg afflicted cells. What does all this mean? It means the light you live in has a lot more to do with cancer than your oncologist was taught.

### Tweet 46
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599048533470248960>

If you do not believe me, never forget this slide below. When you know better, you do better. Unfortunately for cancer patients, your centralized oncologists do not.

### Tweet 47
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1599049251216011264>

I hope you enjoyed lesson #2 on light and why zoo humans who live under artificial light tend to get cancer when wild humans and animals who live under the dark and light of the solar cycles do not. To learn more: [patreon.com/DrJackKruse](http://www.patreon.com/DrJackKruse)

# Thread: Nature is a perfect alchemist for mitochondria; they change sunlight into refined products.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1590879456306298881.html>
**Published:** 2022-10-31T14:09:15.000Z
**Tweets:** 7
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1590879456306298881>

Nature is a perfect alchemist for mitochondria; they change sunlight into refined products.
Nature requires a ransom for living things that do not connect with her chronically that is paid in illness and disease.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1590880214787436545>

Light is refined by molecules, atoms, and isotopes in our cells. The refined product is created from light's energy as it is slowed down & its specific atomic structure is linked to the action spectra of the molecule that interacts with terrestrial sunlight.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1590881561318391809>

Lowered water content lowers the quantum yield in a leaf due to diminished photosynthetic yield. Folate is found in foods and those foods' folate levels vary because of how molecules' action spectra slow terrestrial sunlight into an amount of refined B9 vitamin.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1590882510292287488>

The level of folate can vary in how your brain functions in seasons. Folate helps reduce depressive symptoms in the brain by altering serotonin. Natural folate is needed in the brain for the synthesis of norepinephrine, serotonin, and dopamine.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1590883039923826688>

Tryptophan also creates melatonin and NAD+ in mitochondria. Sunlight also increases melatonin production at the same time. All of them are substrates that have seasonal variations = light creates substrate.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1590883693131964416>

Tryptophan is an aromatic amino acid with a very special action spectra.......that make it a chameleon.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1590883975593353216>

Just look at how light sculpts life........

# Thread: There are 1.8 billion barrels of oil and 117.2 trillion cubic feet of gas in the Upper Ordovician Point Pleasant Formati
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1576369358107906048.html>
**Published:** 2022-08-08T14:23:23.000Z
**Tweets:** 15
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1576369358107906048>

There are 1.8 billion barrels of oil and 117.2 trillion cubic feet of gas in the Upper Ordovician Point Pleasant Formation and Utica Shale of the Appalachian Basin Province.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1576370675022852097>

Ordovician Period, in geologic time, the second period of the Paleozoic Era. It began 485.4 million years ago, following the Cambrian Period, and ended 443.8 million years ago, when the Silurian Period began.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1576370933623029760>

Ordovician rocks have the distinction of occurring at the highest elevation on Earth—the top of Mount Everest.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1576373091143335939>

During the Ordovician period, post Cambrian explosion, a rich variety of marine life flourished in the vast seas and the first plants began to appear on land—before the second largest mass extinction of all time ended the period. This is where the hydrocarbons came from

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1576373972614098944>

Climate change and extinction ended this period. A major ice age is known to have occurred in the southern hemisphere and climates cooled world-wide as a result. No humans were needed. Humans do not cause climate change

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1576375179139170304>

The Ordovician is best known for its diverse marine invertebrates plus photosynthetic red and green algae, primitive fish, cephalopods, corals, crinoids, and gastropods. The only land mass at this time was Pangea. All continents were one

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1576375688688345089>

For most of the Ordovician period, global conditions were as stifling as during the preceding Cambrian; air temperatures averaged about 120 degrees Fahrenheit worldwide, and sea temperatures may have reached as high as 110 degrees at the equator without humans

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1576377439672229889>

85% of species went extinct over the relatively short interval of 1.4 million years leaving the Appalachian basin behind. There was an 80% increase in 02 levels then. O2 constituted only 14% of the atmosphere in the Darriwilian Stage (Middle Ordovician 460-465 mil yrs ago

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1576378863076093952>

02 increased to as high as 24% of the atmosphere by the mid-Katian (Late Ordovician 450-455 million years ago. This oxygen stimulates massive eukaryotic growth = climate change did the Earth good in the end

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1576380176941821953>

Oil & natural gas, remain significant in the Appalachian basin. Both conventional oil and gas continue to be produced in the Appalachian basin, most new wells in the region are drilled in shale reservoirs, such as the famous Marcellus and Utica Shale, to produce natural gas.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1576382321782988800>

Wells in shale are shallow. Deeper into the basin the reserves are massive and untapped built by millions of years of highly power photosynthesis.

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1576383099146944512>

500 million years ago the present-day Australian and North American plates collided. About 500 million years ago the present-day African and North American plates collided to form this hydrocarbon basin

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1576385774677012480>

Buried under U.S. soil lies an estimated 40 billion barrels worth of proven oil reserves that are still untapped today.

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1576399163440259072>

The BEST solar battery on Earth which stores solar energy has already been invented by nature via photosynthesis called a HYDROCARBON molecule.

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1576401764416622592>

For this reason I am quite bullish on oil/BTC (PoW assets) and I believe this is why Buffett has gone uber long on OXY. OXY is expert in pulling oil from the shallow shale basins in the US. The elite are expert at extracting value from taxpayers

The transfer of electrons between proteins is an essential step in biological energy production.

# Thread: Sunday PSA: How to destroy the Savanna hypothesis with brain biochemistry 101 Let's look at the mammalian vegetarian bra
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1553856905377873920.html>
**Published:** 2022-08-02T16:59:58.000Z
**Tweets:** 29
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553856905377873920>

Sunday PSA: How to destroy the Savanna hypothesis with brain biochemistry 101

Let's look at the mammalian vegetarian brain. The best way to see how a brain is constructed is to look at a brain in a terrestrial vegetarian land mammal.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553857306433028098>

They have an 0mega 3 fatty acid called docosapentaenoic acid (DPA) in their non-neural tissues and it is very abundant in their bodies. Their neural membranes, however, hoard DHA and conserve it tremendously. Why is that?

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553857577796214785>

Because DHA is so rare on land food sources, land mammals who were herbivores all have really small brain sizes compared to their body size. Moreover, as their body size got bigger (think elephant as an analogy) they got a logarithmic reduction in brain size.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553857732842774530>

This was also seen in the dinosaurs who were terrestrial too! So we know the environmental food sources have limited terrestrial-based animals up until we showed up on planet earth.

Non-Geeks: They got ‘less smart’.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553857869568786433>

More neural biochemistry insight of Homo’s Solution you were not told in any paleo book: DHA and DPA are both 22 carbon fats, but DPA has only 5 double bonds while DHA has 6. The only chemical difference is one double bond between DHA and DPA.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553859037082656771>

In 600 million yrs of neural evolution, Mother Nature has made no attempt to replace DHA with any other fat. Why?

It suggests to me, that the chemistry of the DHA molecule has a “unique electrical property” that is not fully shared by DPA in the vegetarian mammals' brains.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553859190896082944>

In other words, its use has limited their cerebral development. The moral here? You must have a lot of DHA no matter what dogma you currently carry in your own present beliefs to make a human brain.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553859411017441285>

Deep implication: Just living on the land, eating meat predominately, will not give you enough DHA to make the 3-pound brain we have. You might be beginning to realize then that maybe the ‘paleo solution’ was not really Homo’s original solution at our genesis of speciation.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553859548183764997>

You would also be quite correct in that assumption. It is physically impossible to build a human brain without a steady supply of DHA and brain-specific nutrients and neural biology prove it definitively without any doubt.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553859692904030208>

It's a shame no one looked at the major morphologic difference between transitional ape and man before jumping the gun on what we SHOULD be eating as the base of our food pyramid, huh?

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553859867923939328>

I think the "paleo solution" is what we CAN eat after the Younger Dryas and remain relatively healthy but to say it is optimal is just not factual. What we can eat is not that important when we are talking about brain construction.

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553860021087346688>

What we should be eating should be the question we ask! Just because you can eat a vegetarian template does not mean we should. Since we became human we have developed the ability to eat a wide variety of diets and still survive.

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553860125445836800>

But when we veered off the path of what was optimal for our brain we began to see neolithic disease generation. We have the opportunity to change that if we want to when we consider what the human brain requires for optimal function.

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553860380807544833>

Want more proof my radical idea might be the right solution for homo?

60% of the brain is made of lipids and all brain cell membranes have another particular characteristic.

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553860494175477765>

They have high levels of DHA in them! In fact, in different mammal species, the content of AA and DHA do not vary but their brain size does vary. This tight relationship is broken by us, humans. Ask yourself why this is the case now.

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553860656591413248>

This suggests the genes that control brain growth are tightly related to a deep environmental source of DHA. If you have a neurologic disorder, eating disorder, or psychiatric disorder of any type you might pay close attention to this part of the thread.

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553860785813733378>

It is where you will begin to find the remedies and clues that will heal you from what has ailed you for so long.

Geek alert: DHA is also specifically and selectively incorporated into neural cell membranes and concentrated at synapses between neurons.

### Tweet 18
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553861098805288967>

The synapse is the most unsaturated part of the cell membrane in the brain. That means this is where DHA is needed most. Seed oil damage occurs here when DHA is missing.

### Tweet 19
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553861216862347271>

At the synapse is where chemical and electrical transmission occurs. It is vitally important in the construction of a human brain. Some DHA can be made from ALA but the process is highly rate limited in human biochemistry.

### Tweet 20
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553861446815125504>

Another major issue is that ALA is very susceptible to oxidation, at a rate of 60% in 24 hours, compared to only 5% oxidation for DHA. That is a substantial difference. This is why I tell all my patients "fowl is foul".

### Tweet 21
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553861656299683840>

Even if they eat flax seed it is not the right kind of omega 3 for the human brain. ALA forces competition for the desaturase enzymes so that means if you have a large brain you do not ever want to eat flax of vegetarian sources of omega 3.

### Tweet 22
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553861927767613441>

Moreover, excitable membranes do not work well with oxidized lipids in them. Vegetarian sources of omega 3 are all loaded with the highly oxidizable ALA. This is why you need to avoid them like the plague if you are bright.

### Tweet 23
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553862239316238336>

ALA allows for major oxidation and that is why vegans/vegetarians/fruitarians are at serious risks for neolithic disease generation. We know that cell membrane stability is critical in all neurodegenerative disorders we treat in medicine today.

### Tweet 24
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553862394232946689>

The work of Patricia Kane at John Hopkins is also enlightening in this area. If you are not facile in her work you need to be. If you do not believe that, go talk to someone with Alzheimer’s disease.

### Tweet 25
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553862527955738624>

Today, it is well established that dietary sources of DHA are an independent determinant of brain growth and in evolution across all species. (Broadhurst

### Tweet 26
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553862635560607744>

It goes even further folks; Pierre Budowski at Hebrew University showed definitively that there is direct competition between omega 6 and omega 3 PUFAS and the balance of both in the brain is critical.

### Tweet 27
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553862744448851969>

This is also a tightly controlled system in the human brain. Moreover, as land-based mammals grow, their body plan growth rates outstripped their ability to make DHA endogenously and they had no exogenous supply, so they kept small brains!

### Tweet 28
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553863047319519234>

Small brains are found in shitcoiners, vegans, and vegetarians, and people who support WEF policies of eating bugs and using privacy restricting CBDCs

### Tweet 29
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1553863201934262274>

That is why there is a relationship in all land mammals that the bigger they got the dumber they get.

This implies that DHA is the SINGLE rate limiting to step to a big brain in the entire land food web on this planet. Bye, bye savanna hypothesis!!!
