# Thread: Some believe it is perhaps the most momentous technical advance in history.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1554512340988706817.html>
**Published:** 2022-08-02T14:19:10.000Z
**Tweets:** 30
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554512340988706817>

Some believe it is perhaps the most momentous technical advance in history. Me......not so much. It opened the door to Frankenfoods via processing.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554535945273237506>

Isotopic analysis of the Haber-Bosch synthesis of ammonia versus Nitrogen cycle shows N from HB favors Nitrogen 14 and 15, and that of Nature's N cycle favors N14, N13. That atomic mass thing is big when you consider E=mc^2. Few do.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554536125104103429>

Marine photosynthetic web favors N13 for example. You never see that in the Fritz Haber reaction. Never.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554536479602483201>

The Haber Process, also called the Haber-Bosch Process, is a complex chemical procedure that takes nitrogen from the air and under HIGH pressures and temperatures combines it with hydrogen to produce ammonia. This is why isotopes vary.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554537160719605762>

Increasing the pressure of the reaction increases the yield of ammonia. It also varies the isotopic variables so they do not mimic Nature. How? In Nature, the N-N is a triple bond is very hard to break. It's an irreversible step requires atmospheric lightning to break bond

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554537565637824522>

Due to the Haber process is a reversible reaction, the yield of ammonia can be changed by changing the pressure or temperature of the reaction. this allows isotopic variability. Nature is telling us she wants one isotope of N to make food/chlorophyll/hemoglobin = picture

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554537866654613506>

In the Haber process, nitrogen and hydrogen react together under these conditions: a high temperature - about 450°C. a high pressure - about 200 atmospheres (200 times normal pressure) an iron catalyst = not natural.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554538156657201153>

A lot of exogenous energy is needed to break these bonds and form atoms that can rearrange to form ammonia. This energy is called the activation energy, the energy needed to start the chemical reaction. Therefore the activation energy is high.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554538435595100164>

The 30 million volts in lightning is how Nature runs its Nitrogen cycle as it generates the Schumann resonance. It favors the two lightest isotopes of Nitrogen to become free for biomolecules.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554538953633562625>

Haber process never reaches equilibrium.
At equilibrium only 15% yield of ammonia is achieved however, the reaction conditions can be altered to increase the rate of the forward reaction to increasing the yield of ammonia. As a result of $$$, rxn doesn't attain equilibrium.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554539686047145986>

There are several processes that can affect the isotopic composition of nitrate. For example, microbial fixation of N2 from the atmosphere and subsequent bacterial nitrification (a multistep redox process) produces nitrate of a different isotopic composition (δ15N-NO3−)

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554540075609985024>

This is a big deal for humans' microbiomes. Few microbiome science experiments ever control for this in their studies. They should. Many of the problems around the human gut are due to the wrong isotope in food/groundwater from "Big Pharming"

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554540362697527297>

The industrial production of nitrate fertilizer through the Haber–Bosch &Ostwald processes shows variable Nitrate isotopic composition is determined both by source and by the process it is created.

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554540519144947713>

While atmospheric molecular oxygen & oxygen in water have different isotopic compositions, microbial nitrification and denitrification are processes that fractionate isotopic composition.

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554541180066697219>

Measurement of coupled N and O isotopes in nitrate has been a starting point for source identification of nitrate in groundwater. Farmers and humans should know the implications of using fake nitrogen. Few do. Decentralized MDs learn this stuff.

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554541751016296450>

Let me show you how this one small variable in the atomic mass of Nitrogen can cause us to miss important clinical issues for people in California.

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554541896772554752>

The nitrification of NH4+ to NO3− involves three oxidation reactions: NH4+ to NH2OH, NH2OH to NO2−, and NO2− to NO3−, with each step catalyzed by a separate oxidoreductase or oxidase.

### Tweet 18
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554542041798934541>

The oxygen atoms involved in each step have been shown in multiple laboratory studies to come from two distinct sources: the oxidation of NH4+ derives oxygen from atmospheric O2, while the second and final oxidation steps use oxygen atoms from ambient H2O.

### Tweet 19
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554542238675476481>

Because of this, a hypothetical δ18O for the nitrified NO3− molecule can be calculated using Equation (1) below, where δ18O-H2O is the stable isotope composition of oxygen in soil water present at the time of nitrification:
δ18O-NO3− = 1/3 δ18O-O2 + 2/3 δ18O-H2O

### Tweet 20
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554543043973455872>

In California, local waters (soil seepage, irrigation waters from CAP H2O) usually have δ18O values of −15% to −5%, depending on the source of the water. Although atmospheric O2 is a relatively constant +23.5%.

### Tweet 21
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554543309208576003>

Other Californian studies have shown soil O2 to have a variable range of +23% to +29% (with more enriched values due to fractionation by respiration in soil.)

### Tweet 22
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554543462141370369>

From this data, we should expect δ18O-NO3− resulting from nitrification of ammonium fertilizers and manure to fall between −2.2% and +4.5% in California due to the combination of high δ18O atmospheric oxygen and lower δ18O soil-water oxygen atoms.

### Tweet 23
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554543636255236102>

Synthetic NO3−, a product of the Haber–Bosch process & the Ostwald process, is not produced by this same nitrification reaction, however, and derives its oxygen atoms entirely from
atmospheric O2, resulting in a much heavier range for δ18O-NO3− of +17% to +25%!!

### Tweet 24
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554544191950168064>

Most centralized MDs do not realize this sets patients up for chronically low Vitamin D conversions, GERD, SIBO, and many other autoimmune conditions that we should not see at this latitude normally. Has your doc explained this to you?

### Tweet 25
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554545795311042560>

This is why I have told my tribe avoid foods grown in California if you got gut issues. Has your doc ever explained this to you in this detail? If not guess why? Centralized docs get ten minutes with you for a 15 $ copay. Here is my patient visits.

### Tweet 26
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554546162178334720>

The effects of fractionation and abiotic exchange during nitrification, and especially successive nitrification and denitrification cycles of the same nitrate pool, as well as the potential for microbiota to nitrify in the absence of normal gut water % has implications.

### Tweet 27
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554546592811827200>

Moreover, any shift away from the strict 2:1 model of fractionation we see in Nature, should cause the doc/patient to be furthered explored during work up. The end. Isotopes matter in biology.

### Tweet 28
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554548131349536768>

One last point: Natural conventional ammonia production done on Earth generates vast amounts of the CO2 for plant growth to keep the cycles moving in unison = decentralized. Modern green ideas are ruining this coupling. Greta/WEF are no fan of nature's methods FYI.

### Tweet 29
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554548720137535490>

CO2 is not the enemy in climate. Greta/WEF are. Green ammonia production, which refers to the CO2-neutral production of ammonia (also taking account of the production of H2 and the supply of energy for ammonia synthesis) = dangerous for humans

### Tweet 30
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1554549521039998976>

Greta/WEF/ EU Farmers fight over nitrogen: The emerging area of electrified ammonia production will be ideologically central to achieving a carbon-neutral society, providing renewable electricity is employed = Holland farmer revolt FYI.

# Thread: Sunlight, nature’s key EMF induces nitric oxide production during an injury.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1550906180079329280.html>
**Published:** 2022-07-14T14:37:12.000Z
**Tweets:** 20
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550906180079329280>

Sunlight, nature’s key EMF induces nitric oxide production during an injury. This can be explained by the increase in the bioavailability of nitric oxide (NO) induced by exposure to EMFs in cell types involved in the reparative process.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550906371918397441>

NO shuts off CCO in mitochondria and this stimulus get rid of bad matrix engines. NO also shunts oxygen to better functioning mitochondria. This clears damaged tissues. I do not think medicine realizes how vital this function is in ALS.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550906473731002368>

Indeed, it has been reported that EMFs activate the calcium calmodulin switch (CaM) which leads to increased activity of eNOS production and bioavailable NO in the microcirculation. This same switch is found in blood vessels where melanopsin resides!!!

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550906867601358849>

At this microcirculation level, the NO is able to activate both guanylate cyclase (sGC) and adenylate cyclase (sAC) properly in the blood in those who sustain cervical cord/blood vessel trauma.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550907413682917380>

This also implies getting outside and your skin in sunlight after a trauma induced event may be a wise thing to do since it appears light activation of wound healing is a critical step.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550907859269046273>

It also helps us understand why so many concussed players are light sensitive. They have induced MELANOPSIN dysfunction. nnEMF/blue light exacerbates the issue in motor neurons due to their higher density of mitochondria than sensory nerves

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550908076236095488>

This is why surgeons have found for years that wounds made earlier in the day heal much better than wounds created at night under fake light conditions. In fact, this is why surgeons do elective surgeries beginning so early in the AM.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550908181563441152>

Might this mean that we should expect more ALS in people who work 3rd shifts or people who rarely go out in the sun who have low Vitamin D and A levels in their blood? I believe so.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550908335666372608>

I also think these people will have very low levels of ascorbate in their blood plasma as well to create the neurohormones needed for wound repair. When there is mitochondrial damage cells increase their oxygen levels and this GENERATES excessive ROS which leads to damage.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550908735698227203>

If the trauma is chronic the ROS never resolves = apoptosis of the motor neuron. Motor neurons have more mitochondria than sensory neurons so they are more sensitive to repetive trauma via blood vessels.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550908907937320960>

Defective mitochondrial respiration disrupts ATP production quickest and shoudl effect neurons with higher mitochondrial density. Is this why motor neurons are more effected in disruption of the spinal cords blood barrier to cause melanopsin damage to the vessels there?

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550909184958423040>

Blue light is known to lower beta oxidation and lower ATP production in humans. Blue light also is known to cause melanopsin dysfucntion when the blue light stimulus is chronic. Melanopsin is now known to be in blood vessels since 2014.

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550909278285881346>

Reductions in cellular respiration and ATP production are well documented in ALS. In post-mortem spinal cord of sporadic ALS patients the activity of all ETC complexes, complex I, II, III, and IV was found to be reduced.

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550911333759176705>

In addition, the activity of complexes I and IV were reported to be impaired in skeletal muscle while complex I activity and ATP levels were reduced in lymphocytes of sporadic ALS patients.

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550911435445780480>

Counterintuitively, in fibroblasts obtained from skin biopsies of sporadic ALS patients the mitochondrial membrane potential (MMP) was increased compared to healthy controls.

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550911618325921793>

Fibroblast repair occurs under the direction of red light which makes up 42% of sunlight. There is no red light in modern indoor light.

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550911830171652096>

I think the leaky MMP might be theresponse of a patient not having their skin in the game of nature enough when they suffer chronic repetitive injuries to the head and neck from trauma or light stress (nnEMF/blue light hazard)

### Tweet 18
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550912070421561344>

I believe ALS is a neural trauma that needs our skin in the game of sunlight quickly. I think the motor neuron needs the off switch of terrestrial sunlight to repair the colony defect in the matrix of motor neurons

### Tweet 19
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550912698807902211>

Moreover, most ALS patient can't or don't get enough solar exposure because the environment is so toxic due to electromagnetic pollution which destroys normal wound healing in motor neurons

### Tweet 20
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1550912833788993536>

I hope you enjoyed this quantum viewpoint of ALS. The end.

Explain DXY: 6 currencies underpin it. With Fx chaos, is DXY relevant when fiats are failing at relative rates? DXY is a fiat-to-fiat metric = fiat currencies are on the same sinking ship. The USD is on a higher deck, but it will go down eventually.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547351534278787074>

Euro, Swiss franc, Japanese yen, Canadian dollar, British pound, and Swedish krona underpin it. The DXY index no longer even accurately reflects present-day U.S. trade.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547351829612199938>

The DXY index was established shortly after the Bretton Woods Agreement dissolved in 1973 with a base of 100, and values since then are relative to this base. So how accurate do you think it is right now? Does it mean what we think it means in 2022?

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547361551597928457>

Whenever the DXY gets too strong on a relative basis to its other currencies, it ricochets back into causing US Treasury market chaos. Treasury mkt malfunctions impact both the real & financial economy. The Fed is now disintermediation mkts directly to QT as high as they must

### Tweet 5

By disintermediating the financial system, the JP's Fed can address this inflation & economy differently in the future = Monetary policy can be made far tighter even after “something breaks” in Asia/EU big. This buys them time to deal with chaos.

# Thread: Reality: traditional signal content from bonds that a flat/inverted yield curve always precedes recession may no longer
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1547591042932781057.html>
**Published:** 2022-07-13T22:42:31.000Z
**Tweets:** 25
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547591042932781057>

Reality: traditional signal content from bonds that a flat/inverted yield curve always precedes recession may no longer be accurate because for 15 yrs we've had YC manipulation by QE. Stimulus no longer = response especially as USD debases.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547591691611172865>

The Fed knows inflation > 5% canNOT be halted without a way higher Fed Fund Rate. The FFR of the '70s and 2022 are disparate and this is why the 1970s inflation bears no relation to 2022.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547592236530995200>

If the Fed lets the FFR go up to 4-8% this would cause massive global depression via credit spiral. So this is why they are holding the line on FFR. They want chronic inflation and unemployment to lower debt/GDP to 60-80% to buy them more time with fiat system.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547592764065345537>

QT risk: spiking rates on $3T of floating rate corp loans alone will have a CASCADING EFFECT DOWN THE ENTIRE CAP STACK OF $12.2T CORPORATE DEBT AND might RESULT IN MASSIVE BANKRUPTCIES in S&P and QQQ.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547593448127008772>

We've seen this AM what spiking the short part of the curve did to MS and JPM: QT has harmed short-end of curve and this FLAT/INVERTED YIELD CURVE has EVISCERATE BANK NET-INTEREST MARGINS while in the next few Qs CORPORATE DEFAULTS likely SURGE.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547594001418571776>

Why are CDS spread widening with QT: What happens when you cut off a bank’s NIM lifeblood & saddle it with loan losses at the same time? CREDIT CONTRACTION begins ACROSS ALL LENDING ACTIVITIES & we likely get BANK defaults/BANKRUPTCIES = FDIC issues when balance of payments up

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547594678819643395>

The current Fed QT plan still carries risks that it could devolve into something globally they cannot stop. I worry that these yahoo's do not see it. Fed is trying to slow energy prices with QT+low FFR. IT is obvious if you look at financial and energy equities pricing

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547595310989336577>

I don't think this is sustainable. This is why some oil analysts have 60 price on oil and others have 380 price point for oil. RISKY DISCOUNT RATES are comprised of 2 components: RISK-FREE RATES + CREDIT SPREADS

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547595640632356864>

What comprises the so-called “risky” discount rate, Rr: Rr = Rf + MRP. In words: the “risky” rate = the “risk-free” rate + the market risk premium.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547596314086477832>

Segments of the economy early this year exhibited NEGATIVE discount rates: the further out the cash flows (the longer duration the asset) or the more negative the cash flows, the higher the prices. This was evident in tech equity & DeFi crypto. Why?

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547596852916195332>

Rf has been artificially suppressed by Fed QE. (pegged at ~0 on the short-end and barely above 2% on the long-end) Why does this matter? Because it was a signal that showed inflation was coming in hot due to Fed QE.

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547597547065094151>

Why? Because commodity price inflation in “opex/capex” commodities would cause many degreed economic people (Lacy Hunt) believe the long-end of the yield curve should wake up with these policies. Many bro's bought it too.

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547597901202747403>

What did the Lacy Hunt's really miss in their analysis? That long-term Rf changes by QE would move higher = no way Fed’s could reign inflation in. This is why people like me knew inflation was coming and calling for ATH in equities and bonds was a lazy analysis.

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547598402577260545>

I knew long ago that as Rr goes up = Asset prices have to go down. And the asset prices MOST affected are those “hyper-beta” risk assets in the S&P/QQQ whose performance relies on low/neg Rr

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547599133938106368>

[#BTC](/hashtag/BTC) went down as beta dropped because of a BELIEF of people they were correlated. They are not. [#BTC](/hashtag/BTC) does not react to Rr as stocks do. [#BTC](/hashtag/BTC) went down because people who owned beta equities and DeFi had to sell BTC to raise cash.

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547599863696609285>

There is nothing in [#BTC](/hashtag/BTC) story that fundamentally got worse to cause it to drop. In fact, [#BTC](/hashtag/BTC) news with LN got substantially better as beta collapse occurred. DeFi crypto fell because of bad governance & behavior in leverage rehypothecation of BTC by bad actors

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547600371337506818>

Some of us saw this last year and it become obvious in early 2022. [zerohedge.com/economics/us-p…](https://www.zerohedge.com/economics/us-producer-prices-soar-most-2018-gas-prices-spike)

### Tweet 18
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547601505494056960>

Supply chain disruption supported the spike in commodities because they are inelastic within QE. One should expect commodities to collapse under QT because liquidity changes drives inelastic assets down. All of this has happened while the global factory China is in mothballs

### Tweet 19
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547602242227712000>

My call 18 months ago on inflation was a contrarian call & now has slowly become a global consensus. Now with WTI down to levels pre Ukraine inflation is still raging. The EU-Russia issue will reignite oil prices by Sept due to winter risks they face. Few in the US see it.

### Tweet 20
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547603208717029376>

Steepening in CREDIT RISK PREMIUMS, easily visibly in mortgage rates now

With avg 30-year fixed rates blowing out and 15-year fixed just behind them, the implied CREDIT SPREADS over equivalent tenor Risk-Free Rates are now very tight between both durations.

### Tweet 21
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547603711022600192>

As the curve steepens and HIGHER LONG RISKY RATES continue to discount High Duration Assets including HOUSING/RE, which is the BIGGEST bubble now imploding rapidly every mkt I look.

### Tweet 22
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547604074538737665>

As the curve doesn’t steepen, and the ducks at the long-end are clamoring to fund our government at 3.35% for 30 years, then FEED THE DUCKS AND TERM OUT OUR MATURITIES!

### Tweet 23
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547604754066317316>

OPEC & ESG policies have done to engineer a backwardated oil curve: 1) creating supply insecurity in the front, while 2) creating demand insecurity in the back.

With QT I think the Fed will do the same with USTs. [@BitcoinTina](https://twitter.com/BitcoinTina)

### Tweet 24
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1547607849156501505>

Dollar is being used as a geopolitical weapon sans Russia
When Yen crosses above ~150, Japan will beg JP to buy JGB

When EURO crosses below ~0.90, EU will ask JP to buy ITALY/GERM/SPAN bonds

EM countries will crash. JP flexing with USD to hold down global inflation/BRICS

The end of the thread. I hope you enjoyed.

# Thread: Wrong.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1544829920559468546.html>
**Published:** 2022-07-07T12:09:02.000Z
**Tweets:** 33
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544829920559468546>

Wrong. Get educated on Thorium nuclear power. The USA has a ton of it. You’re only self-sovereign if you run your full node on a miniaturized backyard molten liquid fluoride salt thorium nuclear reactor. Sounds crazy? Read on.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544830430062559233>

Thorium is the cheapest way to create electricity on Earth. In fact, it is the way nature makes geothermal power of vents, Earthquakes, and volcanoes.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544830575843975173>

Thorium is the key element in the Earth's crust that drive geothermal vents everywhere on Earth. It is an unending source of power that drives plate tectonics.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544830953033551874>

Thorium is a very common element in the Earth's crust at 10 parts per million. We have an unending source of it everywhere in the world. It is cheap to mine and its half-life is 14 billion years. This is the same age as the universe. This makes it hardly radioactive at all

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544831250111815680>

Do you know who Glenn Seaborg is? Glenn Seaborg was one of our great scientists who worked in the metallurgic labs during the Manhattan Project. He won a Nobel Prize. He figured out how to make plutonium quickly from uranium to build nuclear weapons.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544831415778508800>

He discovered plutonium in this quest. He also stumbled into a solution for energy production for man using thorium-based nuclear fuels. The problem was the military focus was on building bombs instead of building a cheap source of energy for the planet.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544831587841449984>

The story of the thorium-fueled molten-salt reactor effort at Oak Ridge National Laboratory, strongly motivated by the discovery of a series of books (Seaborg and Loeb (1981), Seaborg and Loeb (1987), Seaborg and Loeb (1993)) written by Glenn Seaborg and Benjamin Loeb

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544832088628772864>

It covered Seaborg’s years as the chairman of the US Atomic Energy Comm. in the 50s. In the last of the books, The Atomic Energy Commission Under Nixon, Seaborg described budget cuts early in the Nixon Administration that led the AEC to curtail research into breeder reactors

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544832299862204416>

Seaborg described in the books how Alvin Weinberg, head of the Oak Ridge National Laboratory and ardent proponent of the thorium-fueled molten-salt breeder reactor (MSBR), argued for the continuation of that line of research because of how cheap energy could be made safely

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544832401402118144>

The problem was that a new type of reactor needed to be built at a time where Nixon removed the US dollar from the gold standard because of a slowing economy. This sunk the manufacturing effort.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544832823864983555>

Seaborg goes on to describe how the MSBR program was canceled in favor of the plutonium-fueled, liquid-metal fast-breeder reactor (LMFBR) but concedes later that that may have been a mistake. (Seaborg and Loeb, 1993, pg.179).

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544832960599310343>

Seaborg saying this in a book is a mouthful because he won the Nobel Prize for discovering plutonium and the high pressure nuclear reactors that are used today in the US for electricity production.

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544833412661448709>

The industrial-military complex put us on the uranium fission path because they wanted plutonium for weapons. Thorium fusion makes more energy but doesn't create plutonium. So they went the wrong way.

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544833781806387203>

Seaborg recognized that for each absorption of a neutron in uranium-233, it would produce more than 2 neutrons. This was the key to its ability to operate as a breeder reactor. Each neutron produced could split another U-233 atom to unleash energy in a nuclear fission rxn

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544833873925783552>

It turns out any nuclear reaction can be sustained as long as the process liberates at least two neutrons. The best chance to make an unending source of energy is a reaction capable of generating anything above 2 neutrons.

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544834062359093248>

Seaborg knew that the key was which elements on the period table produced the most neutrons when they themselves were hit with a neutron (fission) in a reactor. But how much more than two was the question?

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544834265208295424>

The higher the # of neutrons released = the more energy can be harvested from the fission of an atom to make electric power to do work.

### Tweet 18
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544834480795533313>

When Thorium was tested by Seaborg in the Manhattan Project, Thorium was found to be ideal to be a neutron breeder for the nuclear reaction because its fission elaborated 2.35 neutrons per fission split.

### Tweet 19
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544834611544571904>

With its 14 billion year half life, it became the best candidate to make electricity cheaply. The problem was the military was not interested in electric power, they were interested in explosive power.

### Tweet 20
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544835088571142144>

To make massive amounts of electricity from thorium, thorium needs a special reactor to do it. The US has several of these reactors in Oak Ridge. The largest thorium vein deposits in the world are in the USA in Montana.

### Tweet 21
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544835399511572480>

Thorium can create energy longer and more efficiently than any other source of energy on Earth. Seaborg is the guy who figured this out during the Manhattan Project.

This implies it might be the ideal answer to fossil fuels and the climate change debate as well.

### Tweet 22
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544835682107113472>

The C-H bond in fossil fuels has a million times less energy in it than the Thorium nucleus does for fission.

This is why the Earth's core remains energy vibrant given its planetary mass, while Mars's furnace has turned dormant. Its crust is thorium poor compared to Earth.

### Tweet 23
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544836214926315520>

This is why the largest volcano in the solar system is extinct. Seaborg’s interest in fluorination as a separation technique came from the fact that thorium, when exposed to neutrons in a reactor, would absorb some of those neutrons and form small amounts of uranium-233.

### Tweet 24
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544836300985057283>

If the thorium and uranium were chemically converted to tetrafluorides, which is a rather simple chemical step, and if that mixture was further fluorinated to convert the uranium to a hexafluoride, then it would be rather easy to separate uranium from thorium.

### Tweet 25
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544837005716213761>

This path was chosen by the military because it could be used to make plutonium fastest to create a nuclear arsenal while also generating energy. Today, that decision seems foolish

### Tweet 26
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544837131977228288>

When you understand that thorium reactions produce an unending source of heat to create electric power with a much lower toxicity profile.

Thorium LFTR's avoid this risk because they are all run on lower pressure.

### Tweet 27
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544837258842443778>

U-238/Pu-239 has found the most use in liquid sodium fast breeder reactors and CANDU Reactors.

Th-232/U-233 is best suited to molten salt reactors (MSR). These reactors have produced electricity and nuclear bomb material but have the highest risk level of core meltdown

### Tweet 28
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544837628419350529>

Why? Uranium reactors all use higher pressure systems to operate. Thorium can operate in lower pressure systems to liberate massive unending sources of energy from the thorium nucleus.

### Tweet 29
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544837965964365826>

The MSR program was defunded in 1976 after its patron Alvin Weinberg was fired by the Carter Administration in its first year. Weinberg was a student of Seaborg and Wigner after the Manhattan Project. He learned everything these two found while building the bomb.

### Tweet 30
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544838350103781381>

Politics has played a huge role in fission energy. In 1993, Carlo Rubbia proposed the concept of an energy amplifier or "accelerator-driven system" (ADS), which he saw as a novel and safe way to produce nuclear energy that exploited existing accelerator technologies.

### Tweet 31
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544838468907552768>

Rubbia's proposal offered the potential to incinerate high-activity nuclear waste and produce energy from natural thorium and depleted uranium.

### Tweet 32
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544838657441423366>

Recall, that this was under Clinton and his wife was recently involved in selling a lot of uranium stores of the USA to Russia in the Obama administration under the guise of secrecy as Secretary of State. Biden knows all of this since he was VP of Obama. CRIMINALS run DC.

### Tweet 33
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544839022350110724>

A 2011 MIT study concluded that although there is little in the way of barriers to a thorium fuel cycle, with current or near term light-water reactor designs there is also little incentive for any significant market penetration to occur. THE END.

# Thread: Risk happens fast, but contagion goes quicker.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1544318520787361792.html>
**Published:** 2022-07-07T12:09:02.000Z
**Tweets:** 130
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544318520787361792>

Risk happens fast, but contagion goes quicker. Right now under QT the US has stopped withdrawals for the world for USD/UST. This is destroying the eurodollar and petrodollar systems. Brandon made a poor decision using our oil reserves for gas prices. This draw down=WAR?

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544318979333922816>

War is also a version of fiat contagion that usually follows economic calamity. Today we are in the process of fiat failure. The CCP knows the American War machine needs oil to operate. Might they be waiting for the SPR to go critical before they take Taiwan?

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544319768903225347>

This idea mimics the same strategy the Japanese had in WW2 in their rationale for attacking Pearl Harbor. The Japanese made errors in sinking more ships than oil depots and history has shown this was a big error. Cutting off oil to the military is how you limit capability

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544320073355198465>

The CCP has been stockpiling all commodities but especially cheap oil from Russia and it appears they are ramping up to fight in the South China Sea.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544321369894227972>

The need for a national oil storage reserve has been recognized for at least five decades.

Secretary of the Interior Harold Ickes advocated the stockpiling of emergency crude oil in 1944. President Truman's Minerals Policy Commission proposed a strategic oil supply in 1952.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544321682948739076>

Eisenhower suggested an oil reserve after the 1956 Suez Crisis. The Cabinet Task Force on Oil Import Control recommended a similar reserve in 1970.

But few events so dramatically underscored the need for a strategic oil reserve as the 1973-74 oil embargo = Petro$ system

**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544353555892543489>

DG has two special geographic traits:

First, it is at extremely low risk for typhoons.

Second, despite being in the middle of an impossibly-vast body of saltwater, DG naturally produces large sums of its own freshwater.

### Tweet 100
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544353717952122888>

This is a tremendous logistical advantage for supply chains. The island's geological quirks make it home to a number of hydrological lenses, or bodies of freshwater that float on top of saltwater.

DG's lenses are massive.

### Tweet 101
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544353957832802304>

This obviates the need for extensive water purification and storage facilities or constant maritime resupply. Water is a heavy commodity to ship and RO water requires massive hydrocarbon use to do it.

### Tweet 102
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544354224821178368>

A 20' ocean container equipped with a plastic bladder (a "flexitank") will only carry about 24,000 liters of water.

A 40' container with 20 pallets of bottled water will yield about 17,280 liters of water. One person uses about 100L/day. The USN provides cover for trade

### Tweet 103
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544354361572204545>

DG's freshwater resources means more vessel space, more electricity, and more valuable real estate available for other uses than processing and storing potable water.

For a remote military base in the middle of the ocean, this is a critical, highly-desirable advantage.

### Tweet 104
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1544354482431074314>

The data shows you BRICS are amenable to using oil as money to get away from the USD. In parallel, there is an ongoing effort to develop a digital non-banking payment system, which would be linked to gold and other exchange-traded commodities–the ‘stablecoins.’

# Thread: The US played its last “trump card” against Russia.
**Source:** <https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1543657929504706563.html>

**Tweets:** 43
----------------------------------------------------------------------

### Tweet 1
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543657929504706563>

The US played its last “trump card” against Russia. Having “frozen” 625B of Russian foreign exchange reserves in custody accounts of western central banks, US, EU, UK financial regulators, undermined the status of the dollar, euro, & pound as global reserve currencies.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543658161567064065>

This step sharply accelerated the ongoing dismantling of the dollar-based economic world order built around globalization and tied to the WEF idea of one-world governance.

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543658825244344321>

Soon the BRICS will create a monetary unit based on a basket of commodities that demonstrates a high degree of resilience and stability. That won't work because not all sovereigns have commodities and in that world, they would be disadvantaged.

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543659628084568071>

What will emergy is a monetary-based on energy. Ford envisioned this first but Nakamoto built it 13 yrs ago. This unit will insulate the state from money. This feature is why all centralized states will try to run from it.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543659812579430401>

It's equitable because small countries without commodities can use other fuel sources to create the monetary unit with their governments to make better decisions. In this way, the small and compete with the large feasibly.

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543660222031581189>

It's use creates thermodynamic efficiency that is not present in today's gov'ts. It provides a balance and check grid tied to better decision making around energy policy.

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543660726056882177>

Decisions that are energy efficient will be rewarded. This will force all nations toward the the proper policies linking them to the decision making processes by heads of state or legislative branches. Those who decide poorly will be punished. Voters will easily assess it.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543661015203717120>

Currently, the U.S. is fighting to maintain its dominance, but just as Britain previously, which provoked two world wars but was unable to keep its empire and its central position in the world due to the obsolescence of its colonial economic system, it is destined to fail.

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543661270691348480>

The war in Ukraine is likely to facilitate its demise when the western markets realize what a loss of energy liquidity really means to their old world fiat markets this winter.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543661607804354560>

The British colonial economic "energy system" was based on slave labor. It was overtaken by structurally more efficient economic systems of the U.S. and the USSR during the cold war.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543661921664221186>

Both the U.S. and the USSR were more efficient at managing human capital in vertically integrated systems. But neither were energy efficient. This caused a split the world into their zones of influence in the 1960s-70s

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543662253597130752>

The US was more efficient in its decision making when it closed the gold window on their Ponzi fiat, but they built the Petrodollar system to act as another source of deposits to support monetary energy in 1973. This bought them 50 yrs.

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543662633974435842>

The USSR failed completely by 1991 when their system collapsed because economically they lost energy efficiency to sustain the public. This failure lead to the ideas of an energy based monetary unit around hydrocarbons.

### Tweet 14
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543663022820192257>

Putin's economy has been slowly built around improving the Petrodollar system in a more equitable way for Russians. It has hit snags, but his ideas have been the ultimate stress test for the US monetary system. Currently, it is failing the stress test.

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543663405764169728>

Putin knows his version of the energy based monetary unit will not be adopted he just wants a seat at the table to help shape the policy. He got Xi, the USA factory on board with his stress test idea because the CCP is commodity poor. They need a new monetary unit as well.

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543663831032922113>

A transition to a new world economic order started after the disintegration of the USSR by the WEF. Putin studied this plan early on and realized its limitations. It is a new version of the constitutional monarchy that 1917 destroyed in Russia.

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543664130363723776>

He figured out quickly this is why the Germans and Royal families were on board because by history and blood both are linked. Russia was too, but the Bolshevik revolution cut the tie.

### Tweet 18
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543664384324550656>

This transition of the WEF plan is now reaching its conclusion with the imminent disintegration of the dollar-based global economic system, which provided the foundation of the US global dominance post WW2. This mimics what the USSR saw in 1991.

### Tweet 19
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543664814203060224>

The new convergent economic system that emerged in the BRICS is the next inevitable stage of development, combining the benefits of both centralized strategic planning & market economy, & of both state control of the monetary and physical infrastructure and entrepreneurship.

### Tweet 20
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543665083846467584>

This system will be unacceptable to the US, but from an energy standpoint it is more sustainable than the current US system and it will cause that system a rapid decline. More rapid than most think.

### Tweet 21
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543665555097505796>

### Tweet 22
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543665912821194754>

The BRICS won't allow WW3 to happen to get terms of the deal now because they know a kinetic war is not going to give the results they need to be energy efficient. Instead they have chosen to destroy the old system from the outside using its poor design against it.

### Tweet 23
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543666413319094275>

That experiment is playing out macroeconomically now. So far it is not going well for the US. Consensus will likely be driven economically by an energy efficient solution by the countries who join the new world economic order. BTC will be a centerpiece of this system

### Tweet 24
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543666742236512256>

Why? None of the parties have any trust with one another. For money to work trust has to be established for all.

### Tweet 25
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543667111964409856>

In the first phase of the transition, the immediate replacement is national currencies, gold, and Bitcoin.
The countries fall back on using their national currencies and clearing mechanisms, backed by bilateral currency swaps. This phase is almost over.

### Tweet 26
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543667423689285635>

Presently, price formation is mostly driven by prices at exchanges in USD. This phase is almost over: after Russia’s reserves in dollars, euro, pound, and yen were “frozen,” it is unlikely that any sovereign country will continue accumulating reserves in these currencies.

### Tweet 27
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543667955766026245>

The forward looking signals that phase one is over: 10 yr yields will rise due to sovereigns outside the US rejecting USD hegemony, CDS widen, Bitcoin price down allowing accumulation by BRICS before citizens in this countries. This is on going & early.

### Tweet 28
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543668370750545920>

The second stage of the transition will involve new pricing mechanisms that do not reference the dollar. The CCP has a problem. The yuan–won’t be taking the USD place due to its inconvertibility and the restricted external access to the Chinese capital markets.

### Tweet 29
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543668853531615234>

Commodities will take over this space in the middle portion of phase two. This has already begun in Russia. Eventually, commodities will scale back and Bitcoin will ascend because of its trustless qualities. That will mark the beginning of phase three & where BW 3 is headed

### Tweet 30
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543669096595734531>

Gold is a transition-only money for the globe because the use of gold as the price reference is constrained by the inconvenience of its use for payments.

### Tweet 31
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543669241093709824>

The third and the final stage on the new economic order transition will involve a creation of a new digital payment currency founded through an international agreement based on principles of transparency, fairness, goodwill, and efficiency.

### Tweet 32
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543669647500869632>

That transition will first be through commodities like oil and transition to nuclear and geothermal powered money. Eventually, the transition will be full to [#Bitcoin](/hashtag/Bitcoin) when sovereigns have had the time to adapt to its use case and value.

### Tweet 33
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543669933783097345>

I see the BRICS leading the transition. A currency like this can be issued by a pool of currency reserves of BRICS countries, which all interested countries will be able to join.

### Tweet 34
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543670079791013890>

The weight of each currency in the basket could be proportional to the GDP of each country (based on purchasing power parity, for example), its share in international trade, as well as the population (3 billion strong) and territory size of participating countries.

### Tweet 35
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543670424030126081>

The world will be forced to see the plan work by causing the old dollar system to fail under commodity stress tests of inflation. That is the weakest point of the USD/UST system. This is on display now in the world.

### Tweet 36
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543670767472316416>

What will the transition basket look like? the basket might contain an index of prices of main exchange-traded commodities: gold and other precious metals, key industrial metals, hydrocarbons, grains, sugar, as well as water and other natural resources. BTC will be in it

### Tweet 37
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543671110662774790>

As the system evolves [#BTC](/hashtag/BTC) place in the basket will grow because the other commodities are geopolitical and will come to be looked at is energy inefficient during the transition which will be applied experimentally.

### Tweet 38
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543671465517748225>

The transition currency would be used exclusively for cross-border payments and issued to the participating countries based on a pre-defined formula to be equitable with respect to energy inputs.

### Tweet 39
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543671603833225219>

Participating countries would instead use their national currencies for credit creation, in order to finance national investments and industry, as well as for sovereign wealth reserves. Capital account cross-border flows would remain governed by national currency regulations

### Tweet 40
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543671957807415297>

Eventually, the transition of the currency will flow thermodynamically and the basket will evolve as it becomes clear to governments which is the most valuable and trusted asset in the system. Bitcoin will replace almost everything in the basket.

### Tweet 41
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543672343465234434>

That is how I see the next 50 years macroeconomically unfolding. The end. I hope you enjoyed this visit to my brain.

### Tweet 42

**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1543630826771988482>

Master accounts are available only to banks that are members of the Federal Reserve System and other depository institutions, & only if they satisfy additional criteria established by the particular Federal Reserve Bank

### Tweet 2

### Tweet 3

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1529924135304024064>

Someone needs to inform the "Real Mar" “The clock, not the steam-engine, is the key-machine of the modern indus­trial age.” Lewis Mumford said it in 1934. Today Bitcoin is that clock.

### Tweet 5
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1529924550418481158>

### Tweet 6
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1529924975951593480>

### Tweet 7
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1529925255518633984>

A real world physical token directly repre­sents the state of things in reality. The second one , a ledger,  indirectly reflects the state of things.

### Tweet 8
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1529925410905112580>

Tokens are physical and distrib­uted; ledgers are infor­ma­tional & central­ized by design. Tokens are inher­ently trust­less; ledgers are not

### Tweet 9
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1529925717051461633>

Because of these properties, in the digital realm  we can only use ledgers. Shitcoin marketing gurus will lie and say the opposite. See Novogratz or Pal as examples.

### Tweet 10
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1529926389834690561>

10/The digital realm is only an informational realm.

### Tweet 11
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1529926463906107392>

The ledger-like nature of all digital infor­ma­tion is the root cause of the double-spend problem. It is a time problem.

### Tweet 12
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1529926935681417222>

Digital tokens, like all infor­ma­tion, can only be spread, like an idea
if you have an apple and I have an apple, and we swap apples — we each end up with only one apple. But if you and I have an idea and we swap ideas — we each end up with two ideas

Charles Brannan

### Tweet 13
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1529927070679281665>

what we call physical bearer assets, or “cash” — are free from this dilemma. In the real world, if you hand me a coin, your coin is gone.

**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1529927153797808133>

The laws of physics do not allow you to double-spend it.

### Tweet 15
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1529927365169758216>

In the digital realm, because we are always dealing with infor­ma­tion, double-spending is an inherent problem. As everyone who ever copied a file or used copy-and-paste knows

### Tweet 16
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1529927600298246144>

### Tweet 17
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1529927906994044928>

### Tweet 19

### Tweet 1

Participants in Moderna’s clinical trials for its mRNA booster Covid shots keep dying. And Moderna keeps obscuring their deaths.

### Tweet 2
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1516875327087202304>

### Tweet 3
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1516875520138485764>

### Tweet 4
**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1516875769099739136>

The death occurred in August 2021, at least six weeks before the Food and Drug Administration held a hearing in October to discuss authorizing a Moderna booster at a dose lower than the one used in the August trial.

**Source:** <https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1516876076881952771>
